runners.name: Khafar
runners.spotlight: Despite showing inexperience, she shaped quite well in chasing home an impressive winner on her Newbury debut last August; favourite when some way below that level at Kempton on her second start; Wolverhampton run looked too bad to be true, but no excuses were put forward; 24,000gns Shadwell cast-off; risky, but the bar is set low for her Irish debut.
runners.comment: Made an encouraging debut at Newbury, doubts raised by poor AW efforts, Shadwell cast-off

runners.name: Carmel Valley
runners.spotlight: Similar form at the Curragh on her only start at two and on reappearance at Limerick; needs to find extra; better ground may help.
runners.comment: Modest form on only start at two and on reappearance, better ground may help her cause

runners.name: Goomah
runners.spotlight: Some encouragement from her juvenile runs at Naas and Dundalk; despite interference, not beaten far on the second occasion in a race won by Khafar's trainer; could feature in a maiden of modest standard for this venue.
runners.comment: Two fair runs at two, could feature in what looks like an ordinary maiden for this venue

runners.name: Valentino Eclipse
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-brother to three winners, including two 6f juvenile scorers for this stable Thunder Eclipse and Eclipse Emerald (also won in Hong Kong later); unraced dam closely related to 2m Group 3 winner Dee Ex Bee, out of a sister to outstanding Dubai Millennium; hooded for debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to three winners, this looks like a weak maiden for this venue, could feature

runners.name: Caislean Ni Cuan
runners.spotlight: E16,000 yearling; first foal; dam once-raced half-sister to two winners, out of 7f-winning sister to unbeaten champion juvenile Teofilo.
runners.comment: First foal; dam once-raced half-sister to two winners, out of a winning sister to Teofilo

runners.name: Halon Bay
runners.spotlight: Made debut over 1m2f; showed improvement over 1m at Bellewstown but soundly beaten in fourth; several of the stable's Flat and jumps runners have run well recently.
runners.comment: Made debut over 1m2f, showed improvement over 1m at Bellewstown but well beaten in fourth

runners.name: Gilts
runners.spotlight: Not a bad run in midfield at the Curragh on debut; likely to need more experience.
runners.comment: Not a bad run in midfield at the Curragh on debut, likely to need more experience

runners.name: Cosmic Funk
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two Gowran outings in recent weeks; possibly one for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two Gowran outings in recent weeks, possibly one for handicaps after this

runners.name: Threebiggulps
runners.spotlight: Started slowly and never in contention over 1m at Dundalk; down the field over 6f at the Curragh.
runners.comment: No early signs of winning potential in races over 1m at Dundalk and 6f at the Curragh

runners.name: Connecteo
runners.spotlight: Failed to make any impact in two Dundalk outings at two; gelded now; transformation needed.
runners.comment: Failed to make any impact in two Dundalk outings at two, gelded now, transformation needed

runners.name: Cannes
runners.spotlight: Chased home now 100-rated stablemate James J Braddock on debut last year at the Curragh; sent off 11-8 favourite here on seasonal reappearance when keeping on for a 1.5l second (over 4l clear of the third); well worth a go over this far on that evidence, but good ground is an unknown; leading chance if he acts on it.
runners.comment: Solid debut run, shaped like further would suit here latest, chance if acts on good ground
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing & Stall Perlen AB

runners.name: Almazann
runners.spotlight: Promising start to career with a head second at Cork (10.5f, gd-yld) when sent off the 9-4 favourite; step up to this longer trip looks almost certain to suit on breeding; big player.
runners.comment: Promising start when beaten a head at Cork, bred to relish this longer trip, big player
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Antigua
runners.spotlight: Didn't show a whole lot in two starts last year; step in the right direction on seasonal return here last month (1m2f, yld-sft) but was still beaten 6.5l into fourth; longer trip and new cheekpieces need to eke out more.
runners.comment: Modest form last year, step forward on seasonal return, more needed; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Count Bezukhov
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt; unsold for E125,000 as a yearling; dam 1m-11.6f winner (inc 2yo/AW/Group 1 German Oaks; RPR 103), closely related to French 1m winner Mia Sunshine, half-sister to smart 7f-8.6f winner Kingdom Come; nicely bred but McMonagle prefers stablemate.
runners.comment: Wootton Bassett colt, dam German Oaks winner, McMonagle prefers stablemate

runners.name: Dunmore
runners.spotlight: Palace Pier gelding; E33,000 yearling; half-brother to five winners inc Armande (1m2f/10.5f inc Group 2; RPR 113), Ambivalence (1m inc 2yo; 105), Alpine Spirit (7f; 88) and Rapture's Delight (7f; 84); dam 9.5f AW 2yo and 11.5f turf Listed winner (95); plenty to like on paper and worth a market check.
runners.comment: Palace Pier gelding, dam Listed winner, lots to like on paper, check the market

runners.name: Glen Echo
runners.spotlight: 40-1 and never involved on debut at Navan (1m2f, gd-yld) last month; can only be watched upped in trip.
runners.comment: 40-1 and never involved on debut at Navan, can only be watched for now

runners.name: Mighty Wave
runners.spotlight: Modest run on debut at the Curragh (1m2f, sft-hvy); much better last time dropped back to 1m at Gowran when a staying-on 2.75l third of 10; steps up in trip and, given his sire, could improve for faster ground; place claims.
runners.comment: Modest debut run, stayed on well over 1m latest, up in trip, better ground could suit

runners.name: Amethyst Stone
runners.spotlight: 50-1 and always near the back when soundly beaten in a Navan maiden last month; may need more time.
runners.comment: 50-1 and never figured on debut at Navan last month, may need more time

runners.name: City Of Memphis
runners.spotlight: Looked an exciting prospect when bolting up in Cork maiden last May; thrown straight into Group 1 company in the Irish 1,000 Guineas and acquitted herself very well, beaten 4 1/2l into fifth behind Lake Victoria, despite racing keenly; won over this trip at Gowran after a lengthy absence, leaving an impression she can go on to better things.
runners.comment: Won at Gowran after a lengthy absence, left an impression she can go on to better things

runners.name: Snellen
runners.spotlight: Winner of the 2023 Chesham; again ran well at Royal Ascot last year, second in 23-runner Kensington Palace Stakes; sprang a surprise in a four-runner 1m1f Group 3 at Leopardstown on penultimate start; this may be too sharp on reappearance.
runners.comment: 2023 Chesham winner, won Group 3 over 1m1f last season after a fine Royal Ascot second

runners.name: Princess Child
runners.spotlight: Improved throughout the campaign last season, landing a competitive handicap at Galway over 7f and following up in Group 3 company at Tipperary before another good effort when fourth in Group 2 at Longchamp; beaten 6l into seventh at the Curragh on return; much better at the same venue six days later when a long-priced second in a 1m Group 3 event.
runners.comment: Five wins including a Group 3, solid display at a big price at the Curragh last month

runners.name: Carla Ridge
runners.spotlight: Ran well in defeat in Group 3 races on her first two outings last season; out of her depth in the Commonwealth Cup; subsequent stakes race form linked to the smart Sky Majesty was promising, and she ended the campaign with a stylish career-best in winning a 6f Curragh Listed race by 10l; can prosper at this trip.
runners.comment: Ended last season with a stylish career-best in a 6f Listed race, can prosper at this trip

runners.name: California Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner; stand-out turf display was her second to Lake Victoria in Irish 1,000 Guineas (City Of Memphis fifth); failed to go on from that run last season, and now 0-14 on turf; pleasing return to action when landing 1m conditions race at Dundalk; held by Princess Child on last month's Curragh running.
runners.comment: Two wins on AW, 0-14 on turf, held by Princess Child on last month's Curragh running
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Signora
runners.spotlight: Early promise; three instances of beaten favouritism before an odds-on maiden win at Limerick; her chance is not obvious in the light of an official rating of 93; however, dramatic improvement is not out of the question with the stable in outstanding form this week.
runners.comment: Three instances of beaten favouritism before odds-on maiden win, her chance is not obvious
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Mallavelly
runners.spotlight: Progressive in handicaps last year; went up an aggregate 11lb for wins at this venue (1m) and at Tipperary; sixth to Princess Child on her only stakes race outing; ended the season with a fine third in a premier handicap over 1m here.
runners.comment: Progressive in handicaps last year, sixth to Princess Child on her only stakes race outing

runners.name: Fregada
runners.spotlight: Found wanting in two stakes races last year; this is ambitious for her seasonal debut and first attempt at this trip.
runners.comment: Found wanting in two stakes races last year, this is ambitious for her seasonal debut

runners.name: Duckadilly
runners.spotlight: Second in a Listed race at Killarney last year; likely to find this company too demanding, though worth noting she was aimed higher in the Matron Stakes.
runners.comment: Second in a Listed race at Killarney last year, likely to find this company too demanding

runners.name: Quebec
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in Pattern races in Germany last season; useful recruit to a powerful team; however, she is the stable's second-string on this occasion.
runners.comment: Placed twice in Pattern races in Germany last season, useful recruit to a powerful team

races.race_name: Porsche In Support Of Breast Cancer Ireland Amethyst Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Alcantor
runners.spotlight: Classy performer for Andre Fabre; five career wins including three Group 3s and was beaten just 1l in the Group 1 Prix de Moulin on his penultimate start last September; top rated here at 114 and obvious chance if ready after break on stable debut, though might prefer softer ground.
runners.comment: Three-time Group 3 winner, top rated 114, obvious chance if ready after break

runners.name: Excellent Believe
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner signed off last season with excellent placed efforts in a Haydock Group 3 (1m, good) and a Listed event at Craon (1m, very soft); not disgraced behind Docklands in Listed Doncaster Mile on seasonal return; sharper for that and this British raider is respected.
runners.comment: Placed in stakes company final two runs last season, strip fitter for comeback, respected

runners.name: Mutasarref
runners.spotlight: Has won ten of his 30 starts, including four times at this venue at 7f-1m; most recent success came at Cork in October 2024 (1m, yielding) but ran some fine races last season, including when third in this; well behind a couple of these on seasonal return here last month but better expected now with that run under his belt; place chance.
runners.comment: Four course wins, behind a couple of these latest but will strip fitter now, place shout

runners.name: Tokenomics
runners.spotlight: Course handicap winner at 1m1f; cracking effort when 2.5l fourth in Listed Heritage Stakes over C&D (yld-sft) last month when he had three of these behind despite not getting the clearest of runs; acts on good ground and seems to be improving; a bit to find on the figures, but could go very well.
runners.comment: Had three of these behind over C&D latest, a bit to find on figures but could go very well

runners.name: Catalina Delcarpio
runners.spotlight: Won 1m2f filles' maiden here on debut (good); subsequently placed in Navan Group 3 (1m2f, soft) and in Group 2 Ribblesdale Stakes at Ascot (1m4f, gd-fm) when the 2-1 favourite; testing ground may not have suited on seasonal return in the Park Express at the Curragh (1m, sft-hvy); should improve for that and yard red-hot, but needs to prove effectiveness for this trip.
runners.comment: Smart stakes form last season, won maiden over 1m2f here, yard in hot form, 1m a query

runners.name: Mississippi River
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner including a rated race last June over C&D (good); promising effort on seasonal return over C&D when 3l fifth to stablemate The Lion In Winter in the Listed Heritage Stakes, half a length behind Tokenomics and just in front of Lord Massusus; rated 100 and improvement is required.
runners.comment: C&D winner last year, solid effort here on return, rated 100, needs to find a bit more
runners.owner: D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Peter M Brant

runners.name: Omni Man
runners.spotlight: Two wins last season including a Goodwood handicap off 93 (1m4f, good); signed off with a Listed third at Dundalk (10.5f); slowly away and never involved on return at the Curragh (1m2f, soft); this ground much more his bag but not certain that the drop back to 1m will suit and overlooked by McMonagle.
runners.comment: Two wins last season and listed placed at Dundalk, 1m may prove too sharp

runners.name: Lord Massusus
runners.spotlight: Confirmed his liking for this venue when winning a Listed race here last season (Mutasarref a neck behind), his second C&D win; improving for each outing this season and should run his race but a rating of 99 leaves him with a fair amount to find with a few of these rivals.
runners.comment: Two-time C&D winner, improving for each run this season but a fair amount to find

races.race_name: Cashel Palace Hotel Derby Trial Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Pierre Bonnard
runners.spotlight: Beat Shaihann in a 1m Dundalk maiden last August; looked smart on his final two juvenile starts; asserted strongly when beating stablemate Endorsement in the Zetland Stakes (1m2f, good to firm); readily beat A Boy Named Susie on very soft ground in the Criterium de Saint-Cloud; now needs to rebuild his reputation after tame eclipse in the Ballysax
runners.comment: Group 1 winner at two, needs to rebuild his reputation after tame eclipse in the Ballysax

runners.name: Endorsement
runners.spotlight: Chased home the current Derby favourite Benvenuto Cellini on debut at the Curragh; won a maiden at Tipperary before second to Pierre Bonnard in the Zetland Stakes; made the running for Pierre Bonnard at Saint-Cloud before fading; same role in the Ballysax here four weeks ago; proved himself a smart type in his own right, sticking to his task as Pierre Bonnard flopped.
runners.comment: Twice behind Pierre Bonnard last season, good front-running effort in the C&D Ballysax

runners.name: Shaihaan
runners.spotlight: Improved from his debut in a 7f course maiden dominated by Aidan O'Brien when chasing home Pierre Bonnard over 1m at Dundalk; found the competition too hot in the Group 2 Beresford Stakes; made a fine start to the season when beating two subsequent winners in a 1m2f Curragh maiden; not out of place in this company now that he is more mature.
runners.comment: Beat two subsequent winners in a 1m2f Curragh maiden, should hold his own  in this grade

runners.name: James J Braddock
runners.spotlight: Promising Navan debut when strong-finishing 1/2l second over 1m, despite rider losing an iron close home; impressed in beating two stablemates in testing conditions in a 1m Curragh maiden at the beginning of November; got outpaced in the Ballysax on reappearance, kept on late in fifth, around 3l behind Endorsement.
runners.comment: Got outpaced in the Ballysax, kept on late in fifth, around 3l behind Endorsement
runners.owner: Aziz Kheir Aust Partners & Kevin Blake

runners.name: Straight Up
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism in a Listowel festival maiden (7f, heavy); may not have been suited by quick ground in the Group 3 Autumn Stakes at Newmarket; hooded after disappointing seasonal debut; making up the numbers for Ballydoyle here.
runners.comment: Making up the numbers for Ballydoyle here, hooded after disappointing seasonal debut
runners.owner: Flaxman Stables, Mrs Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith

races.race_name: "It's Family Day At Leopardstown" Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Beatific
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly should be capable of building on a debut fifth over 1m here four weeks ago; runner-up Thundering On (beaten favourite then) gave the form a boost when winning a Group 3 event at Naas.
runners.comment: Frankel filly should be capable of building on a debut fifth over 1m here four weeks ago

runners.name: Johanna Walsh
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars filly; cost E460,000 as a yearling; ran green on debut at Thurles last October, showed promise with a staying-on third, 3/4l off runner-up Cape Primrose; reversed form with that rival when third behind a longer-priced stablemate at Gowran a month ago.
runners.comment: Promising when third behind a longer-priced stablemate at Gowran a month ago

runners.name: Pink Coral
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly; finished behind a longer-priced stablemate (who was third) when fifth in a nine-runner C&D maiden last month; improvement on the cards; cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: Behind a longer-priced stablemate on debut, improvement is on the cards, cheekpieces tried
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Believed
runners.spotlight: Half-sister by Frankel to four winners, notably 2022 Melbourne Cup hero Gold Trip; looked raw when fourth on debut in a 1m maiden at Dundalk, beaten around 7l; form has been boosted by wins for the second and third; extra two furlongs should suit; hooded.
runners.comment: Looked raw when fourth on debut in a 1m maiden at Dundalk, form has been boosted

runners.name: Jerpoint Abbey
runners.spotlight: Justify filly was a USD275,000 yearling purchase; not bred with Irish winter heavy ground in mind but made a fair debut in a bumper at Naas in January; reported to have made an abnormal respiratory noise in a bumper at the Punchestown festival; hood added to tongue-tie worn then.
runners.comment: Reported to have made an abnormal respiratory noise in a bumper at Punchestown

runners.name: Madam Justice
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to a middle distance winner in Italy; dam unraced half-sister to very useful Hong Kong performer Stephensons Rocket.
runners.comment: Dam an unraced half-sister to very useful Hong Kong performer Stephensons Rocket

runners.name: My First Rose
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-sister by Camelot to two winners for this stable, Red Vibernum and Pierre Royal; dam, by Dubawi, won two 1m Listed races and is from a smart middle-distance family.
runners.comment: Half-sister by Camelot to two winners, dam by Dubawi, won two 1m Listed races

runners.name: Path To Peace
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-sister to four winners; dam unplaced in France, out of US 1m stakes winning half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Bertolini.
runners.comment: Sixth foal; half-sister to four winners; dam unplaced in France, out of a US stakes winner

runners.name: Wickedly Wootton
runners.spotlight: E360,000 yearling; fourth foal; Wootton Bassett filly is closely related to Steel Tiger who stayed well for two AW wins in Britain; half-sister to two French winners; dam, by Galileo, won the 1m Group 2 Prix de Sandringham for Jean-Claude Rouget, out of Nunthorpe winner Margot Did.
runners.comment: Dam, by Galileo, won the 1m Group 2 Prix de Sandringham for Jean-Claude Rouget, noteworthy
runners.owner: E Tynan & S I Weston & Haras d'Etreham & Riviera Equine

runners.name: Wedding
runners.spotlight: E350,000 yearling; made some late progress when fifth in a seven-runner maiden at Gowran; Wayne Lordan, who rode her then, stays loyal to Pink Coral instead.
runners.comment: Made some late progress when fifth in a seven-runner maiden at Gowran, can improve
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Zitkala Sa
runners.spotlight: Moderate efforts in maidens at Dundalk and Limerick last month; up against it here.
runners.comment: Moderate efforts in maidens at Dundalk and Limerick last month, up against it here

runners.name: Genoah
runners.spotlight: Four wins on AW, has raced only four times on turf; plenty to prove on this surface but has to be taken seriously off this mark, 10lb below AW rating; most recent success in January was in a 1m4f conditions race.
runners.comment: Four wins on AW, has raced only four times on turf; 10lb below AW rating, interesting

runners.name: Diamond Exchange
runners.spotlight: Placed form last year; second in a small-field C&D maiden in May; well beaten in third behind a useful filly at Down Royal; winning favourite in a Dundalk maiden in January; made a satisfactory start to this season when third in a C&D event on yielding-to-soft; last year's form suggests better ground will suit her.
runners.comment: Third in a C&D race on yielding-to-soft, last year's form suggests better ground will suit
runners.owner: Bronsan Racing & H Redknapp & John Breslin

runners.name: Ob La Di
runners.spotlight: Won over this trip at Naas last August on good ground; not suited by heavy ground on her final two starts; in rear on soft on return.
runners.comment: Not suited by heavy ground on her final two starts last season, in rear on soft on return

runners.name: Spanish Rising
runners.spotlight: Placed on the second of two starts in Britain; poor on heavy-ground debut for this yard behind a stablemate; can do better now.
runners.comment: Poor on heavy ground debut for this yard behind a stablemate, can do better now

runners.name: Ben Lawers
runners.spotlight: AW winner over 1m, has a bit to find with Akecheta on running at Cork last time; trainer may have a better chance with Diamond Exchange.
runners.comment: AW winner over 1m, has a bit to find with Akecheta on running at Cork last time

runners.name: Akecheta
runners.spotlight: Gained second heavy-ground win in conditions race at Gowran (7f) last September; yet to win a handicap; well supported in two of her three races this term, including the Irish Lincoln; has not run to expectations, but perhaps premature to write off.
runners.comment: Well supported in two of her three races this term, not yet running to expectations

runners.name: Sonoran
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Tipperary maiden and then second in a Cork conditions race in the first half of his juvenile season; things have gone downhill since then; hard to be confident.
runners.comment: Things have gone downhill since starting his career with a win and a second

runners.name: Candleford Green
runners.spotlight: Touched off at this venue on her only start at two; beaten favourite on her reappearance last spring;  made amends on the second of two starts in the autumn, stuck to her task (despite hanging to the right) to land a heavy-ground maiden over a bit shorter than this at Gowran; fair chance she can pay her way in handicaps this season.
runners.comment: Lighly-raced filly, fair chance she can pay her way in handicaps this season

runners.name: Dark Summit
runners.spotlight: French winner on good-to-soft (1m); shaped promisingly enough on debut for this yard in the Irish Lincoln; confidence dented by two more recent runs.
runners.comment: Fair run in the Irish Lincoln on Irish debut, confidence dented by two more recent runs

runners.name: Glyndwr
runners.spotlight: Has won three of his four races at Dundalk; second over 1m at Bellewstown last August on his final turf run in 2025; respected with his mark unchanged after a claiming race win on AW last November.
runners.comment: Has won three of his four races at Dundalk, second on his most recent turf run, respected

runners.name: Kilcrea Rock
runners.spotlight: Promise in two runs at two, notably when second to the subsequent 2025 Irish Derby runner-up Serious Contender; uneven form last year, best efforts were over further than this.
runners.comment: Second to a smart type over 1m at two, best form last year was over further than this

runners.name: Railwayview Lady
runners.spotlight: Sprang 125-1 shock in a 1m2f Navan maiden last September and proved that was no fluke later in the month when going down narrowly on handicap debut over 1m5f at Down Royal (yielding), still green; struggled on heavy ground on final start; that run can be forgiven.
runners.comment: Struggled on heavy ground on final start last year; that run can be forgiven

runners.name: Truth Be Told
runners.spotlight: Appreciated the step up in trip to 1m4f-plus when winning at Thurles on his final start last season; ex-Dermot Weld; further improvement possible for his new trainer; this may not be far enough to bring out the best in him.
runners.comment: Winner over 1m4f, this may not be far enough to bring out the best in him

runners.name: Servare
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on seasonal return at Navan in March (1m2f, heavy); upset in stalls when beaten 3.75l by Darius Dark off 12lb higher mark at Cork (gd-yld); can give a good account if the cheekpieces have a positive effect.
runners.comment: Won over 1m2f off 12lb lower at Navan, ok run off revised mark latest, cheekpieces on

runners.name: Dreoilin
runners.spotlight: Found just Servare too good when a 7l second on handicap debut at Navan (1m2f, heavy); went even closer when a 0.75l second at the Curragh last time (1m, soft, two of these behind); raised 3lb; good ground and this longer trip are unknowns.
runners.comment: Promise in both handicaps, good ground and 1m4f are queries, not discounted

runners.name: Divine Believer
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden form; finished just ahead of Pass The Buck on handicap debut at the Curragh (1m, soft) last month; dropped 3lb and steps up markedly in trip; still early days but others appeal more.
runners.comment: No show in maidens or on h'cap debut latest, down 3lb but others preferred

runners.name: Keepsgettingbetter
runners.spotlight: Has progressed since tackling handicaps; 0.5l win off 58 at Dundalk in March (1m) followed by a 2.25l win at the same venue in April off 65; up another 9lb now and this is a deeper race; has to prove she is effective on turf and at this longer trip (mixed messages in pedigree).
runners.comment: Raised 16lb for two Dundalk wins at 1m and 1m2f, this deeper, trip and turf queries
runners.owner: Mrs Richard Henry & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Eniac
runners.spotlight: Best of three maiden efforts came here over 1m last October (yielding); bred to be better than an opening mark of 78 and his dam won over 1m4f, so good chance he'll stay this far on handicap debut; yard amongst winners.
runners.comment: Best maiden run came here (1m), should stay this far, bred to be better than opening mark

runners.name: Chestnut Palace
runners.spotlight: Just mild promise in three maiden runs within the space of a month; questionable whether this trip will suit on breeding; likely best watched on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Handicap debutante, just mild promise in maidens, this trip a query on breeding, watch

runners.name: Bay Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Beaten 10l on debut over 1m at Dundalk last November; stepped up in trip and off the mark back there over 1m4f 17 days ago; no reason he won't be effective on turf (dam three-time winner on good ground) and rider takes off a handy 5lb; entered in the Irish Derby.
runners.comment: Off mark stepped up to 1m4f on AW, bred to go on turf, rider takes off handy 5lb

runners.name: Darius Dark
runners.spotlight: Big prices all three maidens but sent off 11-2 on handicap debut at Cork (1m4f, gd-yld) and duly delivered a 0.5l win off 63 (Servare a 3.75l fifth); the third from that race has since hosed up in a maiden; raised 5lb but from a talented family and could be more to come.
runners.comment: Off the mark on h'cap debut latest, up 5lb, from talented family, may be more to come

runners.name: Pass The Buck
runners.spotlight: Didn't cut much ice in maidens but hinted there could be better to come when seventh on handicap debut at the Curragh (1m) on seasonal return; not beaten far upped to 9.5f in Ballinrobe maiden on Friday; moves further up in trip now.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in Ballinrobe maiden on Friday at 9.5f, moves up in trip again

races.race_name: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Manoir De Mirande
runners.spotlight: French bumper winner was in the lead when falling at the last over C&D in December; placed in four of his five starts since and rating has come down from an initial perch of 124; not progressive but form of penultimate second at Limerick has worked out well; races prominently and maybe good ground will help.
runners.comment: In the lead when falling at the last over C&D; placed in four of his five starts since

runners.name: Karlynn
runners.spotlight: Showed promise at Clonmel in March and again when beaten little more than 10l there last time, not quite seeing out 2m4f.
runners.comment: Showed promise at Clonmel in March and again when not quite seeing out 2m4f baclk there

runners.name: Maestro Des Pins
runners.spotlight: It's Gino gelding out of twice-raced half-sister to 11.5f AQPS Flat and useful 2m7f hurdle/chase winner Alto Des Mottes; won 1m4f AQPS race on very soft ground in November.
runners.comment: It's Gino gelding won 1m4f AQPS race on very soft ground in November

runners.name: My Girl Robyn
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat maiden; didn't stay 1m4f latest; hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Modest Flat maiden; didn't stay 1m4f latest; hurdling debut.

runners.name: On A Diamond
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in 2m4f bumper last month.
runners.comment: Tailed off in 2m4f bumper last month

runners.name: Arouet
runners.spotlight: Rare runner over hurdles for top Flat yard; winner and Listed-placed on the level after being runner-up on debut here; ran over 1m2f-1m4f and rated 101; novicey jumping when 20l third on hurdling debut at Gowran in February, long way behind runner-up Manoir De Mirande; better ground will help.
runners.comment: Rated 101 on the Flat; novicey jumping when 20l third on hurdling debut at Gowran

runners.name: Dromana Mist
runners.spotlight: Poet's Word filly; dam 2m hurdle winner, half-sister to winners Adare Prince (2m4f-3m1f chase), Like A Hurricane (2m2f/2m3f hurdle) and Whiskey Hall (3m hurdle), out of 2m-2m4f hurdle winner; 66-1 for Tramore debut, misbehaved at start and lost rider at first.
runners.comment: 66-1 for Tramore debut, misbehaved at start and lost rider at first

runners.name: Feel Like Ash
runners.spotlight: Feel Like Dancing gelding was last of three finishers in four-runner point in March; first start for Paul Nolan.
runners.comment: Last of three finishers in four-runner point in March; first start for Paul Nolan.

runners.name: Laligurash
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners Sirobbie (2m7f-3m2f hurdle), Breezy Kin (2m6f chase) and Dubhain (point); dam well held in bumper and 2m2f/2m4f hurdles, half-sister to smart 2m4f-3m3f hurdle/chase winner Ballycassidy; failed to complete in two points.
runners.comment: Failed to complete in two points.

runners.name: Sestini
runners.spotlight: Territories gelding with nice page; dam 1m6f winner (RPR 79), closely related to winners Shantaram (1m5f Group 3), Forever Now (1m6f Listed) and Gan Amhras (1m 2yo/2,000 Guineas third), out of 1m winner; did a good bit of Flat racing in France, winning on debut over 1m4f (heavy).
runners.comment: Did a good bit of Flat racing in France, winning on debut over 1m4f (heavy).

runners.name: Square Jasmin
runners.spotlight: Hunter's Light gelding; E17,000 Y, E23,000 3yo; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Manipura (German 1m Listed) and Lance Les Des (2m hurdle), out of sister to German 1m Listed winner Madresal.
runners.comment: Hunter's Light gelding; E17,000 Y, E23,000 3yo; first foal;

runners.name: Inchidaly Rua
runners.spotlight: For an 80-1 chance, ran fine when beaten 15l in maiden at Tramore.
runners.comment: For an 80-1 chance, ran fine when beaten 15l in maiden at Tramore

runners.name: Granlahan
runners.spotlight: Big odds and never involved in three maiden hurdle outings.
runners.comment: Big odds and never involved in three maiden hurdle outings

runners.name: Take The Free
runners.spotlight: Brother to point/2m3f hurdle winner That's Nice; dam multiple AQPS Flat and useful 2m/2m2f hurdle winner; 33s, tailed off over slightly shorter trip here last month.
runners.comment: Brother to 2m3f hurdle winner That's Nice; tailed off over slightly shorter trip here

runners.name: Ivebeenkewadesert
runners.spotlight: Was 25-1 for Limerick maiden in March, never a factor and finished tailed off.
runners.comment: Was 25-1 for Limerick maiden in March, never a factor and finished tailed off

races.race_name: Sean Murphy Plant Hire Ltd. Rated Novice Hurdle

runners.name: Royal Soldier
runners.spotlight: Won on handicap debut at Gowran (2m, heavy) in March; hit with a double whammy over 2m4f at Fairyhouse last month, came down at the last when narrowly in front, and got a 5lb hike for his troubles.
runners.comment: Gowran winner fell at the last when narrowly in front at Fairyhouse and got 5lb hike

runners.name: Le Roi Du Livet
runners.spotlight: Touched off in a bumper in the autumn; by far his best maiden hurdle run was when 100-1 third at Naas in January (2m3f, heavy), winner ran well at Cheltenham, second and fourth won maidens next time; hasn't come close to that form since.
runners.comment: Best maiden hurdle run was when 100-1 third at Naas in January; not in that form since

runners.name: Crohan Lady
runners.spotlight: Won a point and placed in a bumper; third on handicap debut at Thurles in February and bounced back to form in first-time cheekpieces when prominent throughout in winning ordinary mares' maiden at Tramore (2m, soft); got 5lb for that; has bits of form on good ground.
runners.comment: Prominent throughout when winning ordinary mares' maiden at Tramore (2m, soft);

runners.name: Thisain'tthat
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to four winners and most of them handled good ground and improved for going up in trip; showed promise more than once in maidens and went through the race well when second on handicap debut at Clonmel, (2m4f, yielding), just unable to peg back a front-runner.
runners.comment: Went through the race well when second on handicap debut at Clonmel, (2m4f, yielding)

runners.name: Shirzan
runners.spotlight: Two runs over 2m on soft in March; placed in Leopardstown bumper and 5-2, won ordinary Limerick maiden hurdle in good style; connections feel she'll go on summer ground.
runners.comment: 4yo won ordinary Limerick maiden hurdle in good style; expected to handle quicker ground

runners.name: Moon Mission
runners.spotlight: Twice third in bumpers, including when 3l off runner-up Le Roi Du Livet at Tramore; ran okay a couple of times in maiden hurdles; third of 15 on handicap debut at Punchestown (2m5f, soft to heavy) in January; beaten a lot farther by same winner when mid-field in recent festival handicap at same venue; drops back in trip.
runners.comment: Third on handicap debut at Punchestown; mid-field in recent festival contest back there
runners.owner: J T O'Connor & M P Gleeson & P M Fortune & A McGonigle

runners.name: Blue Train
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner in Britain; money arrived for him on handicap debut at Clonmel (2m, soft) and he scored by a length under a hand ride; up 10lb.
runners.comment: Well-backed winner on handicap debut at Clonmel (2m, soft), up 10lb for snug success

runners.name: Big Dispute
runners.spotlight: Two previous trainers; struggled after winning a bumper on debut; staged a revival last summer when placed in three maiden hurdles (including over 2m4f here), setting the pace each time; ran well in decent Flat maiden here last month on first start for Kevin Coleman and won't mind good ground; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: Led each time when placed in three maiden hurdles last year; good Flat run here lately

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Divaboriva
runners.spotlight: Winner and Grade 3-placed in bumpers; runner-up in first two hurdle outings, over 2m4f here and when chasing home subsequent Grade 1 winner over extended 2m6f at Thurles; after a break, fifth of 20 in Listed 3m novice handicap at Fairyhouse; good ground an unknown.
runners.comment: Winner and Grade 3-placed in bumpers and has some solid hurdling ground, rated 117

runners.name: Hillhead Runner
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner for Peter Fahey and placed three times in summer maiden hurdles last year for Gavin Cromwell; has moved yards again and returns from layoff.
runners.comment: Bumper winner and placed three times in summer maiden hurdles last year; has moved yards

runners.name: Miss Georgina
runners.spotlight: Some creditable efforts before placing for the first time at Clonmel (2m3f, soft); rated 100.
runners.comment: Some creditable efforts before placing for the first time at Clonmel (2m3f); rated 100.

runners.name: Noble Name
runners.spotlight: Point and good-ground bumper winner; respectable runs in first two hurdles, back from a layoff.
runners.comment: Point and good-ground bumper winner; respectable runs in first two hurdles, off 189 days

runners.name: Golden Wings
runners.spotlight: Was 80-1 when third to smart gelding I Started A Joke over 2m at Punchestown in January; suffered an overreach when pulled up next time; up in trip on much drier ground.
runners.comment: 80-1 when third to smart I Started A Joke at Punchestown; suffered an overreach latest

runners.name: La Cote Fleurie
runners.spotlight: Walzertakt mare cost E13,000 as a yearling; first foal; dam runner-up in French 2m1f hurdle, sister to 1m5f Flat and 2m/2m1f hurdle/chase winner Le Precieux, closely related to French 3m3f Grade 1 chase winner Lagunak, out of French 10.5f winner; 9-4 fav when winning fair Tipperary bumper (2m, good to yielding); off seven months.
runners.comment: Was 9-4 fav when winning fair Tipperary bumper (2m, good to yielding); off seven months.

runners.name: Evelyn
runners.spotlight: Rated 94 after six starts over hurdles; unlikely to contend.
runners.comment: Rated 94 after six starts over hurdles; unlikely to contend.

runners.name: Local News
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-sister to 2m5f chase winner Hollywood Harmon; dam unraced half-sister to winners Bob Lingo (useful bumper and 2m-2m6f hurdle/chase) and Talk The Lingo (2m2f-3m hurdle/chase), out of half-sister to useful 2m-2m5f chase winner Dev.
runners.comment: Homebred by Malinas is half-sister to 2m5f chase winner

runners.name: Eagle Doll
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Mad Brian (smart 2m-2m6f hurdle/chase), Pottlereaghexpress(2m5f chase), Jessie Lightfoot (2m7f-3m2f chase) and Myblueeyedgirl (bumper); dam unraced sister to bumper and useful 2m3f-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Ransboro; 100-1 when saddle slipped on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister to several winners; 100-1 when saddle slipped on hurdling debut.

runners.name: Isdistheone
runners.spotlight: Placed once in seven bumper starts; well held in two spins over hurdles and probably wants farther.
runners.comment: Placed once in seven bumper starts; well held in two spins over hurdles

runners.name: Bethpage Theatre
runners.spotlight: Second foal; half-sister to useful 2m1f-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Drumgill; dam unplaced in bumpers, half-sister to US 2m1f/2m2f jumps winner Drewscourt, out of maiden half-sister to 2m4f-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Maid For Adventure.
runners.comment: Half-sister to useful 2m1f-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Drumgill

runners.name: Drumadeise
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Getaway Katie Mai (bumper/2m hurdle and 1m Flat, RPR 88) and Koultas King (2m4f/2m5f hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to useful bumper and 2m1f-3m1f hurdle/chase winner Control Man.
runners.comment: Diamond Boy half-sister to a couple of hurdle winners

runners.name: Eagle River
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; half-sister to point/2m4f hurdle winner I C U In My Dreams; dam bumper/2m4f hurdle winner, half-sister to useful winners Morning Royalty (bumper and 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase) and Morning Supreme (bumper and 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase); point form is nothing special.
runners.comment: Half-sister to point/2m4f hurdle winner; point-to-point form is nothing special; reserve

runners.name: Rebel Queen
runners.spotlight: Mark of 95 after three runs in maidens; likely to struggle.
runners.comment: Mark of 95 after three runs in maidens; likely to struggle.

runners.name: Ri Ra
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well held in bumpers; same story on last month's hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Big prices and well held in bumpers; same strory on last month's hurdling debut

runners.name: Ann Bulkley
runners.spotlight: Keen both times when fourth in academy hurdles for Ciaran Murphy; off five months and now with 'Mouse' Morris.
runners.comment: Keen both times when fourth in two academy hurdles for Ciaran Murphy; off five months

runners.name: Dceilingcantholdus
runners.spotlight: First reserve; third in a pair of points; tailed off in hunter chase and when 200-1 for hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Third in a pair of points; tailed off in hunter chase and on hurdling debut; reserve

runners.name: Dictionary Corner
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete in two starts for Pat Flynn; huge price when beaten 44l in C&D maiden on first start for new yard.
runners.comment: Failed to complete in two starts for Pat Flynn; huge price when beaten 44l in C&D maiden

runners.name: Darby Hill
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; point winner is closely related to bumper, 2m chase and 2m3f hurdle winner St Cuthbert's Cave; 66-1 for last month's hurdling debut over 2m4f at Clonmel, tailed off.
runners.comment: Point winner was tailed off on last month's hurdling debut over 2m4f at Clonmel; reserve

runners.name: Tillyiver
runners.spotlight: Poor form so far when tongue-tied for a bumper and two maiden hurdles; hood added here.
runners.comment: Poor form so far when tongue-tied for a bumper and two maiden hurdles

races.race_name: Farm And Industrial Spares Mallow Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Bearami Creek
runners.spotlight: Good form last autumn, highlighted by Navan win over 2m3f (yielding) off 90; totally out of sorts in two races last month; second reserve for 5.35 at Downpatrick on Friday
runners.comment: In good form last autumn, totally out of sorts in two races last month

runners.name: Minority Interest
runners.spotlight: Win came at Gowran (2m4f, soft) in October off 86; safely held next two starts, but fair run here over an inadequate 2m last time when 4.75l fifth of 13, best work late on; merits consideration back up in trip.
runners.comment: Gowran winner off 86; fair run over inadequate trip latest; merits consideration

runners.name: Somersby Roost
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; 0-6 over hurdles; decent third of 12 in mares' handicap company at Navan in March (2m, soft); way below that level when tailed off over C&D in a maiden last time (yielding); others preferred.
runners.comment: Point winner was tailed off in a C&D maiden last time

runners.name: Jay Pee M
runners.spotlight: Much improved sent chasing last season, winning twice (including here); sent off favourite over hurdles at Thurles and fences at Leopardstown when below best; again couldn't take advantage of significantly lower hurdles mark here last time (2m1f, yielding) when tailed off; attractive mark but clearly risky.
runners.comment: Unable to take advantage of lower hurdles mark twice, inc' here latest when tailed off

runners.name: Lady Bluebird
runners.spotlight: Notched a second hurdles win at Tramore last time with a well-judged front-running ride from today's rider in first-time blinkers, winning easily by 13l; that success came against the tide of moderate form since her last win in 2024; likelihood is that she finds it hard to repeat the trick.
runners.comment: Good front-running ride won the race latest; form suggests she'll struggle to repeat

runners.name: Galavanting George
runners.spotlight: Solid efforts in opening two handicaps on heavy ground, third at Fairyhouse and runner-up at Gowran; beaten when falling at Bellewstown last time (7-2 fav') on better ground; evidence says he might want it softer.
runners.comment: Solid opening handicap runs on heavy; beaten when falling on better ground last time

runners.name: Swelltime
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over hurdles, with latest win coming at Killarney in July (2m4f, yielding to soft) in a comfortable fashion; not much to shout about in his last three starts; just 4lb above last winning mark if the break has done him good.
runners.comment: Four-time hurdles winner is just 4lb above last winning mark in July; back from break

runners.name: Silent Selector
runners.spotlight: Both hurdle wins have come on good ground; off the mark at Limerick in April last year (2m3f) and went in again at Tramore in August (2m5f) off 5lb lower; jumping issues evident at Tramore in October and soft ground against him at Clonmel last month; interesting back on his preferred surface with a run under his belt.
runners.comment: Both hurdle wins came on good; interesting back on preferred surface with race-fitness

runners.name: Eastmore
runners.spotlight: Super consistent form in autumn; winner of a Down Royal handicap off 90 (2m2f, good to firm) before following that with three runner-up finishes at Listowel, Punchestown, and here at 2m-2m2f; has risen in handicap for not winning; tries this trip for first time; check market now back from a break.
runners.comment: Super consistent form in autumn including a win; check market on return from break

runners.name: Nothing Hectic
runners.spotlight: Consistent form early last season was rewarded with a win Ballinrobe mares' maiden hurdle win (2m7f, good); good spell came to a halt when pulled up at Listowel in September on handicap debut; has run well after breaks before.
runners.comment: Consistent spell rewarded with maiden hurdle win in August; PU on Listowel handicap bow

runners.name: Under Cover
runners.spotlight: Minor promise in maidens at 2m-2m4f; beaten 15l in fifth for Down Royal handicap debut in first-time cheekpieces (now left off); never raced on better than yielding.
runners.comment: Beaten 15l on handicap bow; never raced on better than yielding

runners.name: Fairyland Opera
runners.spotlight: Hurdles win came in a Tramore maiden in August (2m6f, yielding); form tanked after that over fences and hurdles; distant third at Naas when last seen in February (2m, soft to heavy); others of more interest.
runners.comment: Form tanked after easy Tramore maiden win in August; others of more interest
runners.owner: Mrs T K Cooper & Miss Diana Cooper

runners.name: Liceo
runners.spotlight: Three-time French Flat winner but ordinary form over hurdles for this yard; well beaten on AW last time; new headgear tried now.
runners.comment: Three-time French Flat winner but ordinary form over hurdles for this yard

runners.name: Speakasyoufind
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; placed in a point but generally poor form over hurdles; pulled up on chase debut at Fairyhouse on latest in January; cheekpieces tried now.
runners.comment: Reserve; generally poor form over hurdles; PU on chase debut latest; cheekpieces on now

runners.name: Mount Ferns
runners.spotlight: Hurdles win in 2024 came at Listowel (2m, soft) off 91; returned there in September for a staying-on third in same event (following 141-day absence); 6-4 favourite for handicap chase at Killarney when last seen, falling at three out when going okay; lightly raced and could go well.
runners.comment: Hurdles winner has run well after breaks before and is still lightly raced; could go well
runners.owner: K McNamee & Manorplace Ltd & V Casey & F G Doran

runners.name: Rubi Tuesday
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; yet to show winning potential, including on handicap debut last time.
runners.comment: Reserve; yet to show winning potential, including on handicap debut last time

runners.name: Brave Crogha
runners.spotlight: First reserve; high point of 16-race career was 200-1 Galway festival bumper win in 2024; shown nothing like that over hurdles.
runners.comment: Reserve; high point was 200-1 Galway festival bumper win; nothing like that form since

runners.name: Bop Wont Stop
runners.spotlight: Modest point form, but ran well in opening maiden hurdle here last year when third (2m1f, yielding to soft); couldn't get near that level in three subsequent runs; returns from over a year absence and best watched for handicap bow.
runners.comment: Ran well here on rules' bow but nowhere near that level after; best watched after absence

races.race_name: Farm And Industrial Spares Mallow Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Jaadil
runners.spotlight: Progressive in the autumn; runner-up at Navan (2m3f, yielding) before gaining a first win in emphatic fashion over C&D (good); good effort at Sligo last time in October when filling the second spot (2m2f, good to yielding); interesting candidate if fit enough.
runners.comment: Progressive in the autumn; C&D winner and second last time; interesting if fit

runners.name: Maxicourt
runners.spotlight: Best run was when second to the smart Cousin Kate in a Tramore maiden in October; nowhere near that form since, beaten 20l in a Tramore handicap last time; cheekpieces go on now.
runners.comment: Runner-up in a Tramore maiden in Oct' but nowhere near that form since

runners.name: Cooltubrid Eva
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; caused a shock at Limerick last time when winning at the 13th attempt at 33-1 (2m4f, soft to heavy); hard to trust her to follow up off 6lb higher on the balance of form.
runners.comment: Reserve; 33-1 winner at Limerick last time, winning at 13th attempt; unlikely to follow up

runners.name: Shesakindofmagic
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner in 2024 (yielding to soft); returned from an absence in January when no match for subsequent Grade 1 winner Place De La Nation in a Fairyhouse conditions hurdle; poor back there next time, but more promising was her fifth of 16 there last month in a first-time tongue tie; yet to be seen if this opening mark is fair.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; yet to be seen if this opening mark is fair on balance of form

runners.name: Humble Brag
runners.spotlight: Left modest form behind upped to 2m4f at Wexford (good to yielding) for second handicap, still in contention when unseating at the final flight; backed into favouritism on that occasion (85-40, previous shortest price was 25-1); well bred and could be the answer.
runners.comment: In contention before UR at final hurdle last time; fav' that day and could be the answer

runners.name: Claude
runners.spotlight: Wexford maiden hurdle winner on good ground for Joseph O'Brien; three outings for this stable have been disappointing, including when pulled up at Punchestown last time (66-1).
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner for J P O'Brien but three runs for this yard have disapoointed

runners.name: Barryroe Madam
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-21 over hurdles, with the win coming in a Listowel handicap in May last year (3m, good) off 85; lost form in final start for Brian McMahon and made a similarly poor stable debut at Limerick last time.
runners.comment: 1-21 over hurdles; poor in last two runs and needs to bounce back

runners.name: Way Down South
runners.spotlight: Point/maiden hurdle/beginners' chase winner; some more fluent leaps at the final two flights at Limerick (2m6f, soft) last time would have seen him go closer; up 1lb and this trip is probably his minimum, but in the mix.
runners.comment: Lack of fluent jumping cost him at Limerick; up 1lb and trip on short side but chance

runners.name: Coded Welder
runners.spotlight: Placed, including behind some useful types, in maidens on nice ground during the summer, with form up to 3m; tailed off in final two starts for Paul Mulligan on winter ground; these conditions will suit him better on stable debut.
runners.comment: Placed behind some useful types in maidens on nice ground last summer

runners.name: Jisco Du Brem
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner (good) is 0-5 over hurdles; kept on for a 17l third of 13 at Thurles on handicap debut (2m7f, soft to heavy); respectable enough third in a Wexford rated novice hurdle last time when well backed (3-1); weakened that day, so perhaps this trip can help; not dismissed.
runners.comment: Bumper winner has run some decent races over hurdles recently; not dismissed

runners.name: Synchronize
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden showed some ability in maiden hurdles but has been poor in three handicaps; others preferred.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose maiden showed some ability in maiden hurdles but poor in handicap starts

runners.name: Koeur A Prendre
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; has shown very little in his three maidens; hopes rest on handicaps transforming him.
runners.comment: Reserve; poor in maidens; hopes rest on handicaps transforming him

runners.name: Garnetot
runners.spotlight: Well clear of the third when runner-up in a Clonmel maiden in October (2m4f, good); out of contention when a faller at the last hurdle on handicap debut later that month on soft ground at Limerick (2m4f, soft); return to better ground can help.
runners.comment: Out of contention when fell on handicap debut in October; return to better ground helps

runners.name: Coolboy Christy
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth in a novice handicap hurdle at Leopardstown (2m3f, soft to heavy) before a lesser effort at Downpatrick, when fifth of ten (2m2f, yielding to soft); others more likely.
runners.comment: Promising fourth at Leopardstown before a lesser effort last time; others more likely

runners.name: Parkgarve
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 11 starts (nine over hurdles); has shown his best form at Downpatrick; took third there in October, perhaps turned out too quickly at Fairyhouse a day later when beaten 29l; wouldn't be the biggest surprise to see him feature, but others preferred.
runners.comment: 11-race maiden; some okay runs in defeat; not the biggest surprise to see him feature

runners.name: Moylussa
runners.spotlight: Tipperary maiden hurdle winner in 2024 (soft); attempted to make all when fourth over 2m4f here in March (yielding); 15l behind winner of big-field Punchestown handicap last time (2m4f, yielding); might prefer softer ground than this.
runners.comment: Attempted to make all when fourth here in March; may want softer ground

runners.name: Kelly's Cosmos
runners.spotlight: Posted fine efforts at Galway in October (2m, good to yielding) and at Limerick in December (2m, heavy); took a step backwards at Fairyhouse when tailed off; given a break since then; place chance.
runners.comment: Some fine efforts in handicaps; given break since tailed off last time; place chance

runners.name: Brianna Lily
runners.spotlight: First reserve; bumper and hurdles winner but last win came two years ago; precious little shown in recent times.
runners.comment: Reserve; last win came two years ago and precious little shown in recent times

races.race_name: Springfort Hall Country House Hotel Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Doctor Du Mesnil
runners.spotlight: Looked highly promising when winning a 2m3f bumper by 11l here last year (second won Grade 2 over hurdles); career didn't take off as expected over hurdles last season; beaten easily at 2-5 by the Passing Wife at Punchestown; pulled up at Cheltenham last time in a tongue-tie that's now left off; not a rock-solid contender, but the one to beat.
runners.comment: Career didn't take off as expected over hurdles last season but still the one to beat

runners.name: Doctor Elvis
runners.spotlight: Progressive in maiden hurdles on winter ground, notably finishing runner-up at Naas and Limerick over 2m3f; progress stalled on handicap debut at Fairyhouse when tailed off in first-time cheekpieces (retained); place chance if bouncing back.
runners.comment: Progressive in defeat in m'dens until tailed off on h'cap bow last time; must bounce back

runners.name: Jeroboam Machin
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner looked a smart prospect when landing a Grade 2 bumper at the 2024 Dublin Racing Festival (soft); returned from a 793-day absence when tailed off in a Fairyhouse bumper in April; questions now as to how much talent he retains, but may come on for that first run back.
runners.comment: Gr.2 bumper winner in 2024; needs to prove he retains talent after tailed off last time

runners.name: Zambezi Moon
runners.spotlight: Made a winning rules' debut here, keeping on best to win by 3.5l in eight-runner bumper (2m3f, yielding); his 80-1 price that day is explained by his modest point-to-point form in three previous outings; needs to step forward again.
runners.comment: Modest point form; 80-1 winner here on bumper debut latest; needs to improve again

runners.name: Seek A Richer Life
runners.spotlight: Tailed off when running out at the fifth at Fairyhouse (2m4f, yielding to soft) when 150-1; impossible to fancy in added cheepieces.
runners.comment: Tailed off when RO at fifth at Fairyhouse; impossible to fancy

runners.name: Killowen House
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fifth attempt in point-to-points at Quakerstown last month, winning by 5l; others more likely.
runners.comment: Off the mark at fifth attempt in points last time; others more likely.

runners.name: Deerpark Hill
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in opening bumper, but returned from an absence much improved to win a weak Tallow maiden point-to-point in February last year; 14.5l behind in a bumper here and returns from over a year off now.
runners.comment: Point winner has run moderately in bumpers and returns from over a year off

runners.name: Little John
runners.spotlight: Weak form in point-to-points; poor on bumper debut at Clonmel; beaten 25l on hurdles debut (2m4f, yielding) back there last time in this headgear.
runners.comment: Weak point form and well beaten in a bumper; 25l behind over hurdles at Clonmel last time

runners.name: Loyal Praetorian
runners.spotlight: Travelled best of all turning in at Limerick (3m, soft to heavy) but couldn't pick up when it mattered; will be of more interest when entering handicaps.
runners.comment: Couldn't pick up when it mattered at Limerick; will be interesting in h'caps

runners.name: Clone Cross
runners.spotlight: A maiden in points and poor in three maiden hurdles; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: A maiden in points and poor in three maiden hurdles; look elsewhere

races.race_name: Bet With Tote.ie On Racing & Sports Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Three County Star
runners.spotlight: Best effort yet here on latest when a 20l fifth of 13 in a 2m7f maiden hurdle (RPR 97); struggled in two previous handicaps off current mark but both were on soft ground; could go well.
runners.comment: Best effort yet in 2m7f maiden here latest (RPR 97), could go well back in a handicap
runners.owner: John P Coleman & M J Caffrey

runners.name: Orinoco Walk
runners.spotlight: Yet to display winning potential in three bumpers and three maiden hurdles at 2m-2m5f; hard to make a case for and best watched on handicap debut.
runners.comment: No signs of winning potential in maiden hurdles or bumpers, hard to make a case for

runners.name: Head For Home
runners.spotlight: Standout effort a 4.75l second of 20 off this mark at Punchestown last October (2m4f, gd-yld); pulled up on return from a 138-day break at Down Royal latest (3m, good); may have needed that and not discounted.
runners.comment: Runner up off this mark in October, pulled up after break latest, not discounted

runners.name: Millie B
runners.spotlight: Four point wins on testing ground but yet to score under Rules; not a bad run on penultimate in a Limerick mares' handicap hurdle but pulled up in a Down Royal chase latest; ground a worry here.
runners.comment: 0-7 under Rules, four point wins on soft and heavy, ground a worry here

runners.name: Only For Our Man
runners.spotlight: Point winner off the mark in a 2m6f novice handicap (good) at Down Royal last month; 7lb rise for that 7l win looks fair and it is partly negated by rider's 7lb claim; should go well again.
runners.comment: 7lb rise for Down Royal win on latest fair, rider's claim an asset, go well again

runners.name: Annie Button
runners.spotlight: Lowly mark but went close at Naas in March when denied by 0.75l (2m6f, sft-hvy); didn't back that up at Limerick latest; unraced on good ground, pedigree suggests it might suit; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Went close at Naas in March, good ground could suit on breeding, cheekpieces on

runners.name: Toor Moon
runners.spotlight: Long-standing maiden has plenty of placed efforts to his name over fences and hurdles; beaten 9.5l when third of 14 at Downpatrick on penultimate (2m6f, yld-sft); below best in Tramore handicap chase latest; place shout but hard to fancy for win purposes.
runners.comment: 0-24 over hurdles but has placed on multiple occasions, hard to fancy for win purposes

runners.name: Halsafari
runners.spotlight: Veteran is a four-time winner; last win came here over 2m (yld-sft) in November;has mixed hurdling and chasing to no avail since and never won beyond 2m4f; others preferred.
runners.comment: Veteran, course winner over shorter, never won beyond 2m4f, others preferred
runners.owner: John P Bowe & Michael J Bowe

runners.name: Navy Waves
runners.spotlight: Has generally struggled since winning at Galway (22.5f, good) last September; a bit better when a plugging-on third of 16 at Tramore (2m5f, gd-yld) on latest but was 16l behind the winner; longer trip here a plus.
runners.comment: Modest third over 2m5f at Tramore last time, step up in trip here a plus

runners.name: Moonshine Girl
runners.spotlight: Maiden was a decent third over fences at Sligo in October but failed to back that up in two starts the following month; 13l fourth in this last year but had the benefit of a recent run; might need this after a break.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on promising Sligo third, fourth in this last year, had benefit of recent run

runners.name: Larkins Lane
runners.spotlight: Hinted that this sort of trip might suit when staying on for a 12l fifth of 16 at Clonmel (2m3f, soft) in March; poor over 2m4f at Tramore latest, however, and has to prove her effectiveness on good ground.
runners.comment: Hinted stamina test would suit on penultimate, poor latest and unproven on good ground

runners.name: Made In The Woods
runners.spotlight: Won a chase at Limerick in 2023 but 0-13 over hurdles and yet to trouble the judge; cheekpieces go on after a moderate run over fences at Clonmel on latest; 4lb lower in this sphere but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Won a chase in 2023, 0-13 over hurdles and yet to trouble the judge, cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Tantrum Boy
runners.spotlight: Beaten long distances at huge odds in maidens at 2m-2m3f; could improve for this longer trip on handicap debut; check the market.
runners.comment: No impact in maidens, potential improver on handicap debut, check market

runners.name: Hay Baby
runners.spotlight: Point winner; failed to reward backers in first three handicap hurdles; well beaten in Punchestown handicap chase latest (hung left under pressure); a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Failed to reward backers in first three h'cap hurdles, well beaten in h'cap chase latest

runners.name: Appian Way
runners.spotlight: Strike rate of 2-40 over hurdles shows he is hard to win with; no joy since 2022 and no signs of an imminent return to the winners' enclosure in recent starts.
runners.comment: 2-40 over hurdles, no win since 2022, recent form underwhelming

runners.name: Imperial Tune
runners.spotlight: Veteran is 0-9 over hurdles; has placed on a couple of occasions but his best handicap efforts have come on testing ground; rain would boost chance but lacks a recent run.
runners.comment: 0-9, best handicap hurdle runs on heavy, rain would enhance claims, lacks recent run

runners.name: Sallap
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; won at Clonmel in October (2m7f, yielding); in the lead when falling at the last off revised mark at Thurles (2m7f, gd-yld); just 2lb higher now and could go well if ready after break.
runners.comment: Won at Clonmel in Oct', leading when fell at last latest, go well if fit; reserve

runners.name: Clever Court
runners.spotlight: 0-6 and has pulled up the last twice; hopes pinned on new cheekpieces, longer trip and better ground sparking improvement.
runners.comment: 0-6, pulled up last twice, moves up in trip on better ground, cheekpieces on

runners.name: I See Fog
runners.spotlight: First reserve; placed in three of four points but 0-4 under Rules and hasn't looked a winner in waiting.
runners.comment: Placed in points, 0-4 under Rules and no sign of winning potential yet; reserve

runners.name: Ranger Billy
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner was badly out of form last summer when last seen in action and has joined a new yard; can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: Two-time winner, out of form when last seen last summer, new yard, best watched

runners.name: Journey So Far
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; 0-9 under Rules and yet to get any closer than 22l to the winner; longer trip and better ground here, but hard to fancy on form.
runners.comment: 0-9 under Rules, yet to get closer than 22l to the winner, hard to fancy on form; reserve

runners.name: Back The West
runners.spotlight: 0-18 and not at best when last seen at Galway in October; trip and ground fine and new headgear combination tried now; mixed record when fresh; others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-18, off since October, new headgear combo tried, mixed record fresh, others preferred

runners.name: Empire Walk
runners.spotlight: Best maiden effort an 8l third at Naas (2m3f, sft-hvy) when shaped as though further would suit; pulled up on handicap debut at Thurles (2m7f, soft) in November; capable of better but good ground a query.
runners.comment: Pulled up on handicap debut at Thurles, capable of better, good ground a query

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF 4-y-o Fillies INH Flat Race

runners.name: Cinating
runners.spotlight: Stayed on late for third place on Leopardstown debut (soft to heavy); didn't improve on that initial experience when 12.5l fourth of seven in Listed heat at Limerick (soft to heavy); best form of those who have raced, but not a tall standard; maybe better ground will help.
runners.comment: Similar efforts in two bumpers; best form of those who have raced, but not a tall standard

runners.name: Clanokre Lass
runners.spotlight: Poet's Word filly; 4,000euros yearling, 2,700euros 3yo; fourth foal; half-sister to 2m hurdle winners The Blaggard and Jubilee Gold; dam unplaced 2m hurdle, out of unraced half-sister to high-class bumper and 2m2f-3m1f hurdle/chase winner Mount Benbulben; stable's Cinating is of more interest.
runners.comment: Half-sister to two 2m hurdle winners; stable's Cinating is more interesting

runners.name: Jenisha
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Little Empire (7f/1m inc AW; RPR 89) and Red Trail (9.5f-10.3f; 74); dam unraced half-sister to winners Kion (6f) and Rosy Redrum (bumper), out of 1m-1m2f winning half-sister to 1m4f Listed winner Tarakala; tongue-tie on for debut; trainer 2-17 in bumpers last five seasons.
runners.comment: Half-sister to two Flat winners inc' Little Empire (RPR 89); tongue tied for debut
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Andrew Heffernan

runners.name: Solviez Song
runners.spotlight: Newcomer by Success Days is out of unraced half-sister to 1m Flat/2m hurdle winner Lady In Lavender and 2m7f chase winner Lady Kate; tongue-tie on for debut
runners.comment: Dam unraced half-sister to 1m Flat/2m hurdle winner Lady In Lavender; best watched

runners.name: A Stoirin
runners.spotlight: Harzand filly; half-sister to winners Painless Potter (1m Flat, RPR 93; 2m4f hurdle) and Wardens Whisper (2m1f hurdle); dam 9.5f winner (73), half-sister to 2m winner Riggsby, out of 1m 2yo and useful 2m-2m4f hurdle winner; trainer has a good strike rate in bumpers, so worth a look in the market.
runners.comment: Half-sister to two winners; trainer does well in bumpers; check market

runners.name: Aine
runners.spotlight: The Grey Gatsby filly; half-sister to winners Lantry Lady (useful 2m hurdle) and Annie's Dream (French 2m1f hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to bumper/top-class 2m-2m4f hurdle winner Annie Power (dam of smart 2m hurdle winner Mystical Power); looks like the one to beat on debut.
runners.comment: Well-bred filly (half-sister to Lantry Lady); looks like the one to beat

runners.name: Green Me Granny
runners.spotlight: 7,000euros foal, 12,500euros 3yo; eighth foal; half-sister to 2m3f hurdle winner Killian's Garden; unraced dam closely related to 1m1f-12.5f Flat/smart 2m-3m hurdle winner Open Eagle, half-sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner Viane Rose; trainer 7-66 in bumpers last five seasons.
runners.comment: Half-sister to 2m3f hurdle winner Killian's Garden (RPR 118); check market

runners.name: My Girl Rosie
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Tornado Bob (useful 2m4f-2m7f hurdle) and Miss Parkington (bumper); trainer 1-21 in bumpers last five seasons.
runners.comment: Dam unraced half-sister to two winners inc' Tornado Bob; unlikely

runners.name: Tell The Boys
runners.spotlight: Bought for just 1,000euros as 3yo; only 9-1 for decent bumper at Wexford (soft) when ran well against geldings (second won next time); a little keen when held up that day and hood now fitted; definite chance.
runners.comment: Stayed on into fourth at Wexford on debut (soft); added hood needs to get more from her

runners.name: Catofthecanals
runners.spotlight: Last of seven on debut in Limerick Listed bumper (soft to heavy); will need to improve a fair bit on that.
runners.comment: Will need to improve a fair bit on her debut when last of seven in a Listed contest

runners.name: Ribadora
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in Cork academy hurdle (2m2f, good); a lot to find.
runners.comment: Tailed off in Cork academy hurdle debut (2m2f, good); a lot to find

runners.name: Senorita Diana
runners.spotlight: Sent off 100-1 for Punchestown bumper debut, when beaten 31l; much more needed.
runners.comment: Sent off 100-1 for Punchestown bumper debut, when beaten 31l; much more needed

races.race_name: Radnor Hills Water Mares' Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: First Candidate
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who built on her early hurdling promise with a comfortable defeat of a subsequent winner at Haydock (1m7f, good; 1-4); she was entitled to win that and she's entitled to win this, even with a penalty.
runners.comment: Entitled to win at Haydock last time and entitled to win this, even with a penalty

runners.name: Enya
runners.spotlight: Chased home a good horse (who won easily) in a 3yo hurdle at Warwick in November and subsequent efforts, all respectable, have been in good-ground bumpers; tongue-tie added for this return to hurdling and there's probably more to come from her.
runners.comment: Second on hurdling debut in November; respectable since then in bumpers
runners.owner: Sheep As A Lamb Syndicate Gary Gillies

runners.name: Briery Rose
runners.spotlight: Well related, being from a family that has served the Daly yard well; however, early indications are that she'll need more time before fulfilling what potential she has.
runners.comment: Early indications are that she'll need more time before fulfilling what potential she has
runners.owner: Mrs H Plumbly, J Trafford, K Deane

runners.name: Dame In Disguise
runners.spotlight: Very modest form in bumpers and over hurdles, and she's held by Silvereen; now goes in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Very modest form in bumpers and over hurdles, and she's held by Silvereen

runners.name: Silvereen
runners.spotlight: She's probably in need of further as she was keeping on when a 5l third over C&D in March (good); still appeals as one of the better alternatives to First Candidate.
runners.comment: Probably in need of further as she was keeping on when a 5l third over C&D in March

runners.name: Bits Ballou
runners.spotlight: Well behind Silvereen here in March and she fared no better at Newton Abbot when 50-1; couldn't recommend.
runners.comment: Well behind Silvereen here in March and she fared no better at Newton Abbot when 50-1

runners.name: Peace Of Amaron
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far when fifth of six in a juvenile hurdle on debut but they went steady and she was tailed off here the next time at 66-1; opposable.
runners.comment: Questionable what she achieved on debut and was tailed off here next time

races.race_name: Lion Leintwardine Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Shorten Sassy
runners.spotlight: Off a reduced mark she finally showed more when a 3l third over 3m at Southwell last month (good) and she would appear to have found her level; respected.
runners.comment: Off a reduced mark she finally showed more when a 3l third over 3m at Southwell last month

runners.name: Churchman
runners.spotlight: Now 0-20 over hurdles but was bang there in 2m3f/2m6f handicaps (good) in March and when beaten nearly 20l the last time he got pressured up front; unlikely to be far away.
runners.comment: Last race didn't go to plan; respected on previous seconds over 2m3f and 2m6f

runners.name: Briery Butterfly
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has had a few chances now in handicaps and she was on the retreat when departing two out at Southwell (3m, good), where Shorten Sassy ran third; not wildly appealing at the minute.
runners.comment: Maiden who was on the retreat when departing two out at Southwell (3m)
runners.owner: Mrs H Plumbly J Trafford K Deane S Holme

runners.name: Son Of Tyran
runners.spotlight: Beaten 24l and about 20l in his two handicaps but both were over 2m on good to soft, and this Irish point runner-up ran his best race under rules in a good-ground bumper; back on this surface and raised to 3m, he could be capable of a lot better; the market should be enlightening.
runners.comment: Back on good ground and raised to 3m, he should be capable of better

runners.name: Athena's Dream
runners.spotlight: Has failed to raise her profile in two handicaps (blinkered; 2m4f/2m3f) and there are stamina concerns over this longer trip; 5lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Has failed to raise her profile in two handicaps; stamina concerns over this far

runners.name: Slack Alice
runners.spotlight: Maiden under both codes; seemed to appreciate the return to good ground here 18 days ago (2m5f) and having the visor back on was no bad thing; needs a bit more off the same mark but hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Third here 18 days ago; needs a bit more off the same mark but hard to dismiss

runners.name: Joker Jack
runners.spotlight: Has no form claims and being 6lb out of the weights is no help; instantly opposed.
runners.comment: Has no form claims and being 6lb out of the weights is no help; instantly opposed

runners.name: Edna E Mode
runners.spotlight: Runner-up over 2m5f here last April and returned to that sort of form when third over that trip at Exeter (good); she was beaten nearly 7l and is off the same mark here, but perhaps this longer trip is what she needs.
runners.comment: Couple of good runs over 2m5f (including latest) and perhaps needs this longer trip

races.race_name: Radnor Hills Splash Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hall Lane
runners.spotlight: Only 1-13 over fences but is threatening to double his tally, having stayed on for second in the last two of three starts for new yard, latest at Taunton (2m7f, good); possibilities off same mark.
runners.comment: Only 1-13 over fences but is threatening to double his tally; possibilities

runners.name: Redbridge Rambler
runners.spotlight: Capitalised on the step back up in trip to win under Freddie Keighley at Warwick (3m, good) yesterday on his second start back from a break; he would have to be respected if running again today.
runners.comment: Won at Warwick yesterday; he would have to be respected if running again today

runners.name: Zacony Rebel
runners.spotlight: Veteran who bounced back with 33-1 success from well out of the weights over C&D (good) most recently, taking Ludlow record to 2-4; just no certainty to follow up, having been inconsistent for a while.
runners.comment: No certainty to follow up his C&D win, having been inconsistent for a while

runners.name: Sunset Boulevard
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo; scored over 2m6f on hurdles debut and final start for Willie Mullins; had wind surgery since the latest of two dismal efforts for new stable; could prove resurgent on handicap/chase debut.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 6yo who could prove resurgent on handicap/chase debut

runners.name: Nazare
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who completed a 2m4f double over hurdles in November for a good start to his handicap career; not in same form in two completed starts since; chance of revival largely hinges on the switch to fences.
runners.comment: Not in same form in two completed starts since hurdles double; switches to fences

runners.name: Soleil d'Arizona
runners.spotlight: Skelton mare who had excuses (faced stiff task, bled from nose) in notable handicap returned to favoured good ground last time; quite progressive last spring to autumn and looks interesting off a workable mark back down in class.
runners.comment: Skelton mare who is interesting off a workable mark back down in class

runners.name: Pergamon
runners.spotlight: Seems a spring type and he prevailed at Huntingdon (2m) last month on return from break, adding to his wins last May and taking hurdles record to 3-6 (3-3 on good ground in this sphere); respected with further improvement plausible.
runners.comment: Prevailed at Huntingdon last month, taking record on good ground over hurdles to 3-3

runners.name: Ufouria
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner in Ireland; inconsistent results for current stable, taking hurdles record to 0-8; not particularly solid but her reduced mark is attractive judged on peak efforts.
runners.comment: Not particularly solid but her mark is attractive judged on peak efforts

runners.name: Zucayan
runners.spotlight: Suited by good ground; proved resurgent when last seen five months ago, enhancing a strong C&D record that also features wins (off 2lb lower) in this race in 2023 and 2025 (faller in 2024); very solid claims kept to this scenario.
runners.comment: Strong C&D record includes wins in this race in 2023 and 2025 (faller in 2024)

runners.name: Lord Cauvelliere
runners.spotlight: Good-ground bumper scorer last May, comfortably beating a rival who currently has a mark of 115; failed to transfer the form to hurdles in the autumn but may improve now handicapping off just 93; interesting.
runners.comment: Handicap debutant who is interesting on the form of his bumper win last May

runners.name: The Tide Turns
