runners.spotlight: Gained sole hurdles success over four years ago when trained in Ireland; has dismal form for current yard and can be dismissed unless the betting suggests a resurgence is likely.
runners.comment: Dismal form for current yard; best watched

runners.name: Hooper
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful for Nicky Henderson until form went awry; hasn't raced since December 2024 and was sold for £3,500 last May; on a five-year losing spell; best watched unless the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: Fairly useful for Nicky Henderson until form went awry; absent since December 2024

races.race_name: GC Rickards Mares' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Claim Du Brizais
runners.spotlight: Bumper/hurdles winner who has posted mixed results since switched to chasing, best effort when third at Warwick (2m4f, good) on penultimate start; mistake contributed to her defeat there latest; still quite unexposed over this distance and not written off.
runners.comment: Mixed results over fences but is still quite unexposed over this distance
runners.owner: Noel Fehily Racing Syndicate - Claim Du

runners.name: Regal Renaissance
runners.spotlight: Posted best effort of her 2025-26 campaign when neck second, clear of the remainder, at Stratford (2m3f, good) last time; appears capable of going one better, having recorded sole chase success (November 2024) off current mark.
runners.comment: Clear second at Stratford last time and appears capable of going one better

runners.name: Northern Air
runners.spotlight: Regressed in three chase attempts during a brief spell with Dan Skelton; ran encouragingly at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good; 1l second) last time on stable debut and switch back to hurdles; fighting chance off same mark, provided she's as effective returned to fences.
runners.comment: Ran encouragingly last time on stable debut and switch back to hurdles

runners.name: Northern Symphonie
runners.spotlight: Hurdles form has dipped sharply for new yard but has she possibilities provided the combination of first-time cheekpieces, switch to fences and a physical tweak (wind surgery since last run) prompts a revival; back on last winning mark.
runners.comment: Back on last winning hurdles mark; wears new headgear on chase debut

runners.name: Ezmerellda
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner in 2023 for previous yard; form for current stable is far from convincing, most recently well beaten on chase debut; nearly a stone out of the weights back up in grade; new headgear needs to make a massive difference.
runners.comment: Unconvincing form for current yard; well out of the weights

runners.name: Jafimgoso
runners.spotlight: Good-ground bumper winner; disappointing over hurdles, though his handicaps have been at 2m and when he tried this trip once before he was 66-1 in a maiden; could easily be a factor.
runners.comment: His handicaps have been at 2m and when he tried this trip before he was 66-1 in a maiden

runners.name: Snapius
runners.spotlight: He only beat two other finishers at Huntingdon (about 2m3f, good) and the 6lb rise appeared to find him out when behind Midnight View at Warwick; he's back down 2lb but they did claim 3lb off his back last time.
runners.comment: Beat just two finishers at Huntingdon and then behind Midnight View at Warwick

runners.name: Jour d'Orage
runners.spotlight: His best effort in five handicaps was the 9l seventh two runs ago at Wincanton (2m4f, good); unconvincing, especially given his subsequent run at Ffos Las; 1lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: His best effort in five handicaps was the 9l seventh two runs ago at Wincanton

runners.name: Man Of Riddles
runners.spotlight: Regressive on the Flat and tailed off returned to hurdles in March; limited appeal now attempting a handicap (1lb wrong).
runners.comment: Regressive on the Flat and tailed off returned to hurdles in March; handicap debut

runners.name: Midnight View
runners.spotlight: His first run beyond 2m when ridden out to prevail over 2m3f at Warwick last month (good; 6lb lower) and he might well improve again for going further this afternoon; has a lot less to prove than his rivals.
runners.comment: Has a lot less to prove than his rivals after winning over Warwick's 2m3f last time

runners.name: Izzy's Grey
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden over hurdles but he has the odd bit of form, as when beaten 6.5l at Chepstow last time (2m3f, good to soft); previous best efforts were on good ground; can run well.
runners.comment: Has the odd bit of form, as when beaten 6.5l at Chepstow last time

runners.name: Ramaah
runners.spotlight: AW winner over 1m5f; 0-13 over hurdles and hasn't been shaping as though he's about to put that right; attempts a longer trip this afternoon; 2lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: 0-13 over hurdles and hasn't been shaping as though he's about to put that right

runners.name: Steady The Ship
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in three successive chases before finishing tailed off back over hurdles; no appeal.
runners.comment: Pulled up in three successive chases before tailed off back over hurdles; no appeal

runners.name: Rayevka
runners.spotlight: Things didn't fall right in a couple of races in Meydan in the spring over 6f, without her running badly, and she is better judged on her second in a C&D event last September on very soft, when a few of these were behind; leading claims.
runners.comment: Should enjoy the drop in trip and slower ground; leading claims

runners.name: Jawwal
runners.spotlight: Capable sprinter on his day and really hit form at the end of 2025, recording a clear career-best when second in the C&D Prix de l'Abbaye, with a few of these behind; well below form when returning for a Listed event at Deauville last month (behind Ponntos) so needs to rebound in a big way with a tongue-tie on.
runners.comment: Good chance on his Prix de l'Abbaye second but no show on his return this year

runners.name: Mgheera
runners.spotlight: Won this race last year on her seasonal return/stable debut and then went on to land the Group 2 Temple Stakes; ran creditably in some top-level events after, albeit behind a couple of these in the Prix de l'Abbaye, and can clearly go well fresh, so makes appeal.
runners.comment: Won this race last year, so can go well fresh; makes appeal
runners.owner: Lord Lloyd Webber And Arthur Mitchell

runners.name: Beautiful Diamond
runners.spotlight: Seems to handle most underfoot conditions and gained a career-best when landing a 5.5f Listed event last year at Ayr on soft ground; ran creditably on her seasonal return at Bath and in the mix if stepping forward from that.
runners.comment: Soft ground will suit and trip is fine; in the mix if improving from seasonal debut

runners.name: Rogue Lightning
runners.spotlight: Does plenty of his racing overseas, and has been over this C&D a few times; hasn't proved the most consistent, but smart when he's at his best and blinkers go back on for first time since October 2024; no problems with the trip but ground would be a concern if it rode genuinely very soft.
runners.comment: Good on his day but genuinely very soft ground would be a concern

runners.name: Estepona
runners.spotlight: Smart sort at his best but beaten as odds-on market leader twice this year already at Fontainebleau, the first time beaten a nose by Lesslepasser; should run well and place chances but hasn't yet won at Listed level let alone a Group race.
runners.comment: Could get into the places but not yet won at Listed level let alone a Group race

runners.name: Afjan
runners.spotlight: Gets the W.F.A allowance and comes here having run a couple of solid races in the spring, but was behind plenty of these in the Prix de l'Abbaye as a juvenile; has at least won a Group event (over this C&D), though, and could have a say for a place at least with trip and ground no issue.
runners.comment: Needs improvement but won't lack for race-fitness

runners.name: Lesslepasser
runners.spotlight: Exposed sprinter who started the season off with a bang when beating Estepona narrowly in March, but has since been well beaten, most recently over 6f at Chantilly in a conditions event; another in the field who hasn't won at Listed or Group level yet.
runners.comment: Capable on his day but hasn't yet won a Listed event or Group race

runners.name: Ponntos
runners.spotlight: Twice winner of this race (2022 and 2024) and you pretty much know what you'll get from him every time, going off in front and attempting to stay there; probably didn't need to be at his best to win a Listed race 14 days ago but Hollie Doyle, who rode him to be fourth in last year's Prix de l'Abbaye, is an interesting jockey booking.
runners.comment: Comes here after a Listed win two weeks ago; might be hard to catch

runners.name: Composing
runners.spotlight: Kept busy as a juvenile but won Group races over 7f at Leopardstown and the Curragh; below best in the Fillies' Mile and similar story on her reappearance (probably needed the run) behind 1,000 Guineas winner True Love; worth a try over this trip and claims if back to the pick of her 2yo form.
runners.comment: Dual Group-winning 2yo but below best on last two runs; claims up in trip if back to best

runners.name: Gilded Prize
runners.spotlight: Created a fine impression and kept unbeaten record intact when bolting up in the Group 3 Cleopatre over 10.5f at Saint-Cloud last month; is now the 2-1 joint-favourite for the Diane and it'll take a good performance to stop her from going in again.
runners.comment: Unbeaten and exciting filly; best effort when bolting up in the Cleopatre; the one to beat

runners.name: Concorde Agreement
runners.spotlight: Stepped up a fair way on form of debut win (AW) at Chantilly when making all in the Group 3 Vanteaux over 1m1f last month; up again in trip/grade but capable of better and seems sure to give a good account.
runners.comment: Won both starts, including the Group 3 Vanteaux last month; should give a good account

runners.name: Evita
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin debut winner of newcomers race at Chantilly in September and ran to a similar level when touched off over same C&D in conditions event on reappearance last month; up in trip but needs to raise game.
runners.comment: Ran to a similar level on both starts but will have to step up if she's to win this
runners.owner: Bradley Thoroughbreds LLC, O Ghrghar & C & Y Lerner

runners.name: Lapotheose
runners.spotlight: Another unbeaten filly, her three wins coming at Angers and Toulouse; more to do in this stronger grade but she's capable of better and no surprise to see her run well; gets cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Won all her three starts, including in Listed race last time; more to do in this company
runners.owner: Riviera Equine S A R L & Maurice Lagasse

runners.name: Habibi
runners.spotlight: Has won two of five starts and turned in best effort when second to Arkansas in Listed event at Saint-Cloud in March; however, that rival was beaten over 6l by Gilded Prize in the Cleopatre so this one will have to post a clear pb to win this.
runners.comment: Best effort in Listed event in March but held by Gilded Prize on line through Arkansas

runners.name: Puerto Rico
runners.spotlight: Ran eight times as a 2yo and didn't win until his sixth start, but that was the Group 2 Champagne Stakes (7f, good) before completing a Group 1 double last autumn with a bit to spare, including the Prix Jean-Luc Lagardere over 7f here on very soft, beating a few of these; usually goes from the front and stable firing on all cylinders now, so sets a good standard if he's trained on.
runners.comment: Dual Group 1 winner who can go from the front; the one to beat if he's trained on
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Komorebi
runners.spotlight: Progressive colt who got off the mark at Clairefontaine last summer and showed some very useful form after; recently recorded a clear career-best when winning the C&D Prix de Fontainebleau from Nighttime and Elastic on good ground, suggesting he's improved over the winter; William Buick on for the first time but never raced on very soft, should it ride that way.
runners.comment: Won the C&D Prix de Fontainebleau last time; William Buick on for the 1st time

runners.name: Nighttime
runners.spotlight: Showed lots of good form as a juvenile, winning three of his five starts, including a win over Komorebi here in a 7f soft-ground Group 3;  bettered that  when second to Puerto Rico in the Group 1 Prix Jean-Luc Lagardere; returned to action with a second to Komorebi in the Prix de Fontainebleau and owner's retained rider on rather than Elastic, but stall 13 doesn't look helpful.
runners.comment: Has plenty going for him on form but stall 13 could be troublesome

runners.name: Dorset
runners.spotlight: Beat current Derby favourite Benvenuto Cellini as a 4-9 shot when breaking his maiden at the Curragh on his second start, and went on to land the valuable Goffs Million over 7f at Leopardstown in September before a Group 3 success; disappointed when seventh on his seasonal reappearance last month, but retains plenty of potential for better if this first try over a mile sparks him back to his best; Moore seems to prefer Puerto Rico though.
runners.comment: Solid second string for the O'Brien team; may improve for a mile

runners.name: Hankelow
runners.spotlight: British raider who won two of his three starts as a 2yo, getting off the mark on debut at York before chasing home a smart sort in the Listed Flying Scotsman Stakes and then landing the Group 3 Autumn Stakes (1m, good to firm) October; likes to race on the front-end but very soft ground would be an unknown.
runners.comment: Smart 2yo who makes his reappearance; can go from front but unproven on very soft
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Elastic
runners.spotlight: Smart performer, who had some good form as a 2yo, beating Mijas last September, and progressed over the winter, landing a mile Listed event on his return before finishing third behind Komorebi and Nighttime in the Prix de Fontainebleau over C&D last month; not dismissed on the figures.
runners.comment: Good run last time over C&D, but others look easier to like for the win

runners.name: Segall
runners.spotlight: Recorded an impressive debut at Saint-Cloud last June with a wide-margin victory over 7f on good ground, and improved a lot on the figures this year, most recently with a victory in a 1m1f Group 3 on good to soft ground here; could step up again but stall 12 not helpful.
runners.comment: Looks to have plenty of ability but stall 12 could be awkward

runners.name: Mijas
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a Chantilly AW conditions event last November and recently won another race of that type, this time over C&D last month on his return to turf, beating a talented rival with lots in hand having moved strongly in behind; this is an altogether different task but certainly one of the more interesting longshots.
runners.comment: Impressed when winning last time and certainly one of the more interesting longshots

runners.name: Wootton Centurion
runners.spotlight: Six-length winner on debut at Chantilly last September (1m, good), and followed that up by winning a conditions event at the same track (1m, soft) from a promising colt on his 3yo return; gone from the front both times and has a fine attitude under pressure; should have lots more to offer and cheekpieces go on, so only issue to overcome is stall 11.
runners.comment: Won both starts and beat a promising type last time; wide draw but could be anything

runners.name: Khovikhov
runners.spotlight: Won a couple of times as a 2yo and recently posted a career-best when runner-up in a mile Listed event on good to soft at Toulouse last month; this represents a completely different test, however, and he is likely to find a few too strong.
runners.comment: Won a couple of times last year but likely to find this too tough

runners.name: Imperior
runners.spotlight: Won a Saint-Cloud maiden over 6.5f last September, beating Khovikhov, but made little improvement after until producing a career-best, again at Saint-Cloud, in March; capable of better and unexposed at the trip but this looks a hard task.
runners.comment: Capable of further improvement but faces hard task on the figures

runners.name: Good Bye Manu
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at Cholet last year but bumped into plenty of these in the past with no great success, so would need a big pb to have any claims of landing a Group 1.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown anything to suggest he can win at this level
runners.owner: Mme Geraldine Reille-Villedey, Butel & Beaunez Et Al

runners.name: Rayif
runners.spotlight: Smart juvenile who won his first two starts, including the Group 3 Prix Francois Boutin (7f, good to soft) at Deauville before losing his unbeaten record when third behind Puerto Rico and Nighttime in the Group 1 Prix Jean-Luc Lagardere (7f) here in October on very soft; fully respected on his return, with a mile likely to suit.
runners.comment: Makes his reappearance after good 2yo campaign; first run over a mile

runners.name: Diamond Necklace
runners.spotlight: Took her record to 3-3 and had Cristophe Soumillon waxing lyrical following her win in last year's Marcel Boussac, where she had Green Spirit and Narissa in second and third respectively; not far off the best of her generation and her trainer reports she's done well physically over the winter; well drawn in stall 3 and will likely be hard to beat.
runners.comment: One of the best of her generation as a juvenile and comes here with leading claims
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: Half Sovereign
runners.spotlight: Progressive as a juvenile, her best effort coming when landing the Group 3 Miesque over 7f on soft in October; disappointed at Deauville on her reappearance and this is much tougher.
runners.comment: Group 3-winning juvenile but disappointed on her reappearance; more needed

runners.name: Zanthos
runners.spotlight: Disappointed when beaten at prohibitive odds at Leicester on second juvenile start but flanked that run with wins at both Newmarket tracks, most recently the Group 2 Rockfel where she had the run of the race against the favoured near rail; may do better but could find it harder to dominate this time.
runners.comment: Group 2 Rockfel winner last year but will find it harder to dominate at this level

runners.name: Showna
runners.spotlight: Caused a shock when beating the short-priced market leader My Highness (subsequently finished ninth in the 1,000 Guineas) in the Imprudence; that form way in advance of anything she'd previously achieved; needs another pb to win in this company back at 1m.
runners.comment: Caused a shock when winning the Imprudence but another pb is needed if she's to follow up

runners.name: Isle Of Fernandez
runners.spotlight: Useful juvenile who turned in a career best when third to Showna in the Imprudence on her reappearance; plenty more needed if she's to cut it at this level, though.
runners.comment: Respectable reappearance run but more needed if she's to figure in this company

runners.name: Green Spirit
runners.spotlight: Progressive last year, winning first four starts (including twice in Group company) and best effort when chasing home Diamond Necklace in the Marcel Boussac; bit disappointing on reappearance when only fourth in the Grotte and, although she's worth another chance, stall 14 could be a hindrance.
runners.comment: Disappointed on reappearance but claims if reproducing form of Marcel Boussac second place

runners.name: Narissa
runners.spotlight: Smart sort who finished a fine third to Diamond Necklace and Green Spirit in the Marcel Boussac and shaped well over C&D on reappearance when chasing home Karl Burke's subsequent 1,000 Guineas runner-up Evolutionist; likely to go well.
runners.comment: Fine third in the Marcel Boussac and shaped well on reappearance; player

runners.name: Baklawa
runners.spotlight: Has won three of nine starts, including in Listed company but she wasn't at her best in the Imprudence last month and she'll do well to figure in this grade from stall 13 draw.
runners.comment: Wasn't at her best in the Imprudence last month and needs to raise game by some way

runners.name: Anashkaya
runners.spotlight: Has won two of seven starts (both wins at Chantilly) but has shown improved form on last two starts, in Listed event on AW and when sixth in the Grotte last time (behind Narissa and Green Spirit); good deal more needed.
runners.comment: Has shown improved form on her last two starts but this demands another big jump forward

runners.name: Ymeeelih
runners.spotlight: Useful form to win newcomers event at Saint-Cloud in March and in conditions event over C&D last time; big step forward needed if she's to win this from stall 12, though.
runners.comment: Has shown useful form on both starts but considerably more needed this time

runners.name: Fashion City
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner at Le Mans in March and, although she showed useful form in a conditions event (just behind Ymeeelih) last month, she'll need a big pb if she's to figure.
runners.comment: Useful sort who will need a big pb if she's to figure in this company
runners.owner: Frantisek Hruby, Michael Motschmann Et Al

runners.name: Maintain
runners.spotlight: Debut 1m1f winner at this venue but turned in best effort when third to Saint-Alary hopeful Concorde Agreement in last month's Group 3 Vanteaux; capable of better, but she's back in trip and will need a step forward if she's to land this.
runners.comment: Best effort when third in the Vanteaux last time; minor place claims back in distance

runners.name: The Last Dance
runners.spotlight: Won both her starts at Chantilly (AW) and Longchamp (beat Ymeeelih and Fashion City) and again gets the services of Christophe Soumillon; needs to step up on the bare facts but is well drawn and no surprise to see her give a good account.
runners.comment: Has won both her starts and needs to improve but could give a good account
runners.owner: Omar Esmil Sh Ghrghar, C &Y Lerner & Antoine Gilibert

runners.name: Radiant
runners.spotlight: Reasonable strike-rate but is an exposed sort who needs to step up significantly if she's to figure from the widest draw.
runners.comment: Exposed sort who will have to step up considerably if she's to win from the widest draw

runners.name: Venosa
runners.spotlight: Ensured a decent gallop for Diamond Necklace at Leopardstown and in the Marcel Boussac last year and was beaten at odds-on in a Galway maiden when last seen in autumn; she'll likely make the running but she makes no appeal.
runners.comment: Useful in her own right but her role is likely to act as pacemaker for Diamond Necklace
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Naughty Peter
runners.owner: Westminster Race Horses SP Z O O

runners.name: May It Be
runners.owner: Thierry Lohest Racing & Breeding & Thierry Storme

runners.name: Rayevka
runners.spotlight: Things didn't fall right in a couple of races in Meydan in the spring over 6f, without her running badly, and she is better judged on her second in a C&D event last September on very soft, when a few of these were behind; leading claims.
runners.comment: Should enjoy the drop in trip and slower ground; leading claims

runners.name: Jawwal
runners.spotlight: Capable sprinter on his day and really hit form at the end of 2025, recording a clear career-best when second in the C&D Prix de l'Abbaye, with a few of these behind; well below form when returning for a Listed event at Deauville last month (behind Ponntos) so needs to rebound in a big way with a tongue-tie on.
runners.comment: Good chance on his Prix de l'Abbaye second but no show on his return this year

runners.name: Mgheera
runners.spotlight: Won this race last year on her seasonal return/stable debut and then went on to land the Group 2 Temple Stakes; ran creditably in some top-level events after, albeit behind a couple of these in the Prix de l'Abbaye, and can clearly go well fresh, so makes appeal.
runners.comment: Won this race last year, so can go well fresh; makes appeal
runners.owner: Lord Lloyd Webber And Arthur Mitchell

runners.name: Beautiful Diamond
runners.spotlight: Seems to handle most underfoot conditions and gained a career-best when landing a 5.5f Listed event last year at Ayr on soft ground; ran creditably on her seasonal return at Bath and in the mix if stepping forward from that.
runners.comment: Soft ground will suit and trip is fine; in the mix if improving from seasonal debut

runners.name: Rogue Lightning
runners.spotlight: Does plenty of his racing overseas, and has been over this C&D a few times; hasn't proved the most consistent, but smart when he's at his best and blinkers go back on for first time since October 2024; no problems with the trip but ground would be a concern if it rode genuinely very soft.
runners.comment: Good on his day but genuinely very soft ground would be a concern

runners.name: Estepona
runners.spotlight: Smart sort at his best but beaten as odds-on market leader twice this year already at Fontainebleau, the first time beaten a nose by Lesslepasser; should run well and place chances but hasn't yet won at Listed level let alone a Group race.
runners.comment: Could get into the places but not yet won at Listed level let alone a Group race

runners.spotlight: Gets the W.F.A allowance and comes here having run a couple of solid races in the spring, but was behind plenty of these in the Prix de l'Abbaye as a juvenile; has at least won a Group event (over this C&D), though, and could have a say for a place at least with trip and ground no issue.
runners.comment: Needs improvement but won't lack for race-fitness

runners.name: Lesslepasser
runners.spotlight: Exposed sprinter who started the season off with a bang when beating Estepona narrowly in March, but has since been well beaten, most recently over 6f at Chantilly in a conditions event; another in the field who hasn't won at Listed or Group level yet.
runners.comment: Capable on his day but hasn't yet won a Listed event or Group race

runners.comment: Won both starts, including the Group 3 Vanteaux last month; should give a good account

runners.comment: Ran to a similar level on both starts but will have to step up if she's to win this
runners.owner: Bradley Thoroughbreds LLC, O Ghrghar & C & Y Lerner

runners.spotlight: Another unbeaten filly, her three wins coming at Angers and Toulouse; more to do in this stronger grade but she's capable of better and no surprise to see her run well; gets cheekpieces.

runners.name: Habibi
runners.spotlight: Has won two of five starts and turned in best effort when second to Arkansas in Listed event at Saint-Cloud in March; however, that rival was beaten over 6l by Gilded Prize in the Cleopatre so this one will have to post a clear pb to win this.
runners.comment: Best effort in Listed event in March but held by Gilded Prize on line through Arkansas

runners.name: City Of Memphis
runners.spotlight: Looked an exciting prospect when bolting up in Cork maiden last May; thrown straight into Group 1 company in the Irish 1,000 Guineas and acquitted herself very well, beaten 4 1/2l into fifth behind Lake Victoria, despite racing keenly; won over this trip at Gowran after a lengthy absence, leaving an impression she can go on to better things.
runners.comment: Won at Gowran after a lengthy absence, left an impression she can go on to better things

runners.name: Snellen
runners.spotlight: Winner of the 2023 Chesham; again ran well at Royal Ascot last year, second in 23-runner Kensington Palace Stakes; sprang a surprise in a four-runner 1m1f Group 3 at Leopardstown on penultimate start; this may be too sharp on reappearance.
runners.comment: 2023 Chesham winner, won Group 3 over 1m1f last season after a fine Royal Ascot second

runners.name: Princess Child
runners.spotlight: Improved throughout the campaign last season, landing a competitive handicap at Galway over 7f and following up in Group 3 company at Tipperary before another good effort when fourth in Group 2 at Longchamp; beaten 6l into seventh at the Curragh on return; much better at the same venue six days later when a long-priced second in a 1m Group 3 event.
runners.comment: Five wins including a Group 3, solid display at a big price at the Curragh last month

runners.name: Carla Ridge
runners.spotlight: Ran well in defeat in Group 3 races on her first two outings last season; out of her depth in the Commonwealth Cup; subsequent stakes race form linked to the smart Sky Majesty was promising, and she ended the campaign with a stylish career-best in winning a 6f Curragh Listed race by 10l; can prosper at this trip.
runners.comment: Ended last season with a stylish career-best in a 6f Listed race, can prosper at this trip

runners.name: California Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner; stand-out turf display was her second to Lake Victoria in Irish 1,000 Guineas (City Of Memphis fifth); failed to go on from that run last season, and now 0-14 on turf; pleasing return to action when landing 1m conditions race at Dundalk; held by Princess Child on last month's Curragh running.
runners.comment: Two wins on AW, 0-14 on turf, held by Princess Child on last month's Curragh running
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Signora
runners.spotlight: Early promise; three instances of beaten favouritism before an odds-on maiden win at Limerick; her chance is not obvious in the light of an official rating of 93; however, dramatic improvement is not out of the question with the stable in outstanding form this week.
runners.comment: Three instances of beaten favouritism before odds-on maiden win, her chance is not obvious
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Mallavelly
runners.spotlight: Progressive in handicaps last year; went up an aggregate 11lb for wins at this venue (1m) and at Tipperary; sixth to Princess Child on her only stakes race outing; ended the season with a fine third in a premier handicap over 1m here.
runners.comment: Progressive in handicaps last year, sixth to Princess Child on her only stakes race outing

runners.name: Fregada
runners.spotlight: Found wanting in two stakes races last year; this is ambitious for her seasonal debut and first attempt at this trip.
runners.comment: Found wanting in two stakes races last year, this is ambitious for her seasonal debut

runners.name: Duckadilly
runners.spotlight: Second in a Listed race at Killarney last year; likely to find this company too demanding, though worth noting she was aimed higher in the Matron Stakes.
runners.comment: Second in a Listed race at Killarney last year, likely to find this company too demanding

runners.name: Quebec
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in Pattern races in Germany last season; useful recruit to a powerful team; however, she is the stable's second-string on this occasion.
runners.comment: Placed twice in Pattern races in Germany last season, useful recruit to a powerful team

runners.name: Puerto Rico
runners.spotlight: Ran eight times as a 2yo and didn't win until his sixth start, but that was the Group 2 Champagne Stakes (7f, good) before completing a Group 1 double last autumn with a bit to spare, including the Prix Jean-Luc Lagardere over 7f here on very soft, beating a few of these; usually goes from the front and stable firing on all cylinders now, so sets a good standard if he's trained on.
runners.comment: Dual Group 1 winner who can go from the front; the one to beat if he's trained on
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.spotlight: Showed lots of good form as a juvenile, winning three of his five starts, including a win over Komorebi here in a 7f soft-ground Group 3;  bettered that  when second to Puerto Rico in the Group 1 Prix Jean-Luc Lagardere; returned to action with a second to Komorebi in the Prix de Fontainebleau and owner's retained rider on rather than Elastic, but stall 13 doesn't look helpful.

runners.comment: Won both starts and beat a promising type last time; wide draw but could be anything

runners.name: Khovikhov
runners.spotlight: Won a couple of times as a 2yo and recently posted a career-best when runner-up in a mile Listed event on good to soft at Toulouse last month; this represents a completely different test, however, and he is likely to find a few too strong.
runners.comment: Won a couple of times last year but likely to find this too tough

runners.name: Imperior
runners.spotlight: Won a Saint-Cloud maiden over 6.5f last September, beating Khovikhov, but made little improvement after until producing a career-best, again at Saint-Cloud, in March; capable of better and unexposed at the trip but this looks a hard task.
runners.comment: Capable of further improvement but faces hard task on the figures

runners.name: Good Bye Manu
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at Cholet last year but bumped into plenty of these in the past with no great success, so would need a big pb to have any claims of landing a Group 1.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown anything to suggest he can win at this level
runners.owner: Mme Geraldine Reille-Villedey, Butel & Beaunez Et Al

runners.name: Rayif
runners.spotlight: Smart juvenile who won his first two starts, including the Group 3 Prix Francois Boutin (7f, good to soft) at Deauville before losing his unbeaten record when third behind Puerto Rico and Nighttime in the Group 1 Prix Jean-Luc Lagardere (7f) here in October on very soft; fully respected on his return, with a mile likely to suit.
runners.comment: Makes his reappearance after good 2yo campaign; first run over a mile

races.race_name: Porsche In Support Of Breast Cancer Ireland Amethyst Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Alcantor
runners.spotlight: Classy performer for Andre Fabre; five career wins including three Group 3s and was beaten just 1l in the Group 1 Prix de Moulin on his penultimate start last September; top rated here at 114 and obvious chance if ready after break on stable debut, though might prefer softer ground.
runners.comment: Three-time Group 3 winner, top rated 114, obvious chance if ready after break

runners.name: Excellent Believe
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner signed off last season with excellent placed efforts in a Haydock Group 3 (1m, good) and a Listed event at Craon (1m, very soft); not disgraced behind Docklands in Listed Doncaster Mile on seasonal return; sharper for that and this British raider is respected.
runners.comment: Placed in stakes company final two runs last season, strip fitter for comeback, respected

runners.name: Mutasarref
runners.spotlight: Has won ten of his 30 starts, including four times at this venue at 7f-1m; most recent success came at Cork in October 2024 (1m, yielding) but ran some fine races last season, including when third in this; well behind a couple of these on seasonal return here last month but better expected now with that run under his belt; place chance.
runners.comment: Four course wins, behind a couple of these latest but will strip fitter now, place shout

runners.name: Tokenomics
runners.spotlight: Course handicap winner at 1m1f; cracking effort when 2.5l fourth in Listed Heritage Stakes over C&D (yld-sft) last month when he had three of these behind despite not getting the clearest of runs; acts on good ground and seems to be improving; a bit to find on the figures, but could go very well.
runners.comment: Had three of these behind over C&D latest, a bit to find on figures but could go very well

runners.name: Catalina Delcarpio
runners.spotlight: Won 1m2f filles' maiden here on debut (good); subsequently placed in Navan Group 3 (1m2f, soft) and in Group 2 Ribblesdale Stakes at Ascot (1m4f, gd-fm) when the 2-1 favourite; testing ground may not have suited on seasonal return in the Park Express at the Curragh (1m, sft-hvy); should improve for that and yard red-hot, but needs to prove effectiveness for this trip.
runners.comment: Smart stakes form last season, won maiden over 1m2f here, yard in hot form, 1m a query

runners.name: Mississippi River
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner including a rated race last June over C&D (good); promising effort on seasonal return over C&D when 3l fifth to stablemate The Lion In Winter in the Listed Heritage Stakes, half a length behind Tokenomics and just in front of Lord Massusus; rated 100 and improvement is required.
runners.comment: C&D winner last year, solid effort here on return, rated 100, needs to find a bit more
runners.owner: D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Peter M Brant

runners.name: Omni Man
runners.spotlight: Two wins last season including a Goodwood handicap off 93 (1m4f, good); signed off with a Listed third at Dundalk (10.5f); slowly away and never involved on return at the Curragh (1m2f, soft); this ground much more his bag but not certain that the drop back to 1m will suit and overlooked by McMonagle.
runners.comment: Two wins last season and listed placed at Dundalk, 1m may prove too sharp

runners.name: Lord Massusus
runners.spotlight: Confirmed his liking for this venue when winning a Listed race here last season (Mutasarref a neck behind), his second C&D win; improving for each outing this season and should run his race but a rating of 99 leaves him with a fair amount to find with a few of these rivals.
runners.comment: Two-time C&D winner, improving for each run this season but a fair amount to find

runners.name: Diamond Necklace
runners.spotlight: Took her record to 3-3 and had Cristophe Soumillon waxing lyrical following her win in last year's Marcel Boussac, where she had Green Spirit and Narissa in second and third respectively; not far off the best of her generation and her trainer reports she's done well physically over the winter; well drawn in stall 3 and will likely be hard to beat.
runners.comment: One of the best of her generation as a juvenile and comes here with leading claims
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: Half Sovereign
runners.spotlight: Progressive as a juvenile, her best effort coming when landing the Group 3 Miesque over 7f on soft in October; disappointed at Deauville on her reappearance and this is much tougher.
runners.comment: Group 3-winning juvenile but disappointed on her reappearance; more needed

runners.name: Zanthos
runners.spotlight: Disappointed when beaten at prohibitive odds at Leicester on second juvenile start but flanked that run with wins at both Newmarket tracks, most recently the Group 2 Rockfel where she had the run of the race against the favoured near rail; may do better but could find it harder to dominate this time.
runners.comment: Group 2 Rockfel winner last year but will find it harder to dominate at this level

runners.name: Showna
runners.spotlight: Caused a shock when beating the short-priced market leader My Highness (subsequently finished ninth in the 1,000 Guineas) in the Imprudence; that form way in advance of anything she'd previously achieved; needs another pb to win in this company back at 1m.
runners.comment: Caused a shock when winning the Imprudence but another pb is needed if she's to follow up

runners.name: Isle Of Fernandez
runners.spotlight: Useful juvenile who turned in a career best when third to Showna in the Imprudence on her reappearance; plenty more needed if she's to cut it at this level, though.
runners.comment: Respectable reappearance run but more needed if she's to figure in this company

runners.name: Green Spirit
runners.spotlight: Progressive last year, winning first four starts (including twice in Group company) and best effort when chasing home Diamond Necklace in the Marcel Boussac; bit disappointing on reappearance when only fourth in the Grotte and, although she's worth another chance, stall 14 could be a hindrance.
runners.comment: Disappointed on reappearance but claims if reproducing form of Marcel Boussac second place

runners.name: Narissa
runners.spotlight: Smart sort who finished a fine third to Diamond Necklace and Green Spirit in the Marcel Boussac and shaped well over C&D on reappearance when chasing home Karl Burke's subsequent 1,000 Guineas runner-up Evolutionist; likely to go well.
runners.comment: Fine third in the Marcel Boussac and shaped well on reappearance; player

runners.name: Baklawa
runners.spotlight: Has won three of nine starts, including in Listed company but she wasn't at her best in the Imprudence last month and she'll do well to figure in this grade from stall 13 draw.
runners.comment: Wasn't at her best in the Imprudence last month and needs to raise game by some way

runners.name: Anashkaya
runners.spotlight: Has won two of seven starts (both wins at Chantilly) but has shown improved form on last two starts, in Listed event on AW and when sixth in the Grotte last time (behind Narissa and Green Spirit); good deal more needed.
runners.comment: Has shown improved form on her last two starts but this demands another big jump forward

runners.name: Ymeeelih
runners.spotlight: Useful form to win newcomers event at Saint-Cloud in March and in conditions event over C&D last time; big step forward needed if she's to win this from stall 12, though.
runners.comment: Has shown useful form on both starts but considerably more needed this time

runners.name: Fashion City
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner at Le Mans in March and, although she showed useful form in a conditions event (just behind Ymeeelih) last month, she'll need a big pb if she's to figure.
runners.comment: Useful sort who will need a big pb if she's to figure in this company
runners.owner: Frantisek Hruby, Michael Motschmann Et Al

runners.name: Maintain
runners.spotlight: Debut 1m1f winner at this venue but turned in best effort when third to Saint-Alary hopeful Concorde Agreement in last month's Group 3 Vanteaux; capable of better, but she's back in trip and will need a step forward if she's to land this.
runners.comment: Best effort when third in the Vanteaux last time; minor place claims back in distance

runners.name: The Last Dance
runners.spotlight: Won both her starts at Chantilly (AW) and Longchamp (beat Ymeeelih and Fashion City) and again gets the services of Christophe Soumillon; needs to step up on the bare facts but is well drawn and no surprise to see her give a good account.
runners.comment: Has won both her starts and needs to improve but could give a good account
runners.owner: Omar Esmil Sh Ghrghar, C &Y Lerner & Antoine Gilibert

runners.name: Radiant
runners.spotlight: Reasonable strike-rate but is an exposed sort who needs to step up significantly if she's to figure from the widest draw.
runners.comment: Exposed sort who will have to step up considerably if she's to win from the widest draw

runners.name: Venosa
runners.spotlight: Ensured a decent gallop for Diamond Necklace at Leopardstown and in the Marcel Boussac last year and was beaten at odds-on in a Galway maiden when last seen in autumn; she'll likely make the running but she makes no appeal.
runners.comment: Useful in her own right but her role is likely to act as pacemaker for Diamond Necklace
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

races.race_name: Cashel Palace Hotel Derby Trial Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Pierre Bonnard
runners.spotlight: Beat Shaihann in a 1m Dundalk maiden last August; looked smart on his final two juvenile starts; asserted strongly when beating stablemate Endorsement in the Zetland Stakes (1m2f, good to firm); readily beat A Boy Named Susie on very soft ground in the Criterium de Saint-Cloud; now needs to rebuild his reputation after tame eclipse in the Ballysax
runners.comment: Group 1 winner at two, needs to rebuild his reputation after tame eclipse in the Ballysax

runners.name: Endorsement
runners.spotlight: Chased home the current Derby favourite Benvenuto Cellini on debut at the Curragh; won a maiden at Tipperary before second to Pierre Bonnard in the Zetland Stakes; made the running for Pierre Bonnard at Saint-Cloud before fading; same role in the Ballysax here four weeks ago; proved himself a smart type in his own right, sticking to his task as Pierre Bonnard flopped.
runners.comment: Twice behind Pierre Bonnard last season, good front-running effort in the C&D Ballysax

runners.name: Shaihaan
runners.spotlight: Improved from his debut in a 7f course maiden dominated by Aidan O'Brien when chasing home Pierre Bonnard over 1m at Dundalk; found the competition too hot in the Group 2 Beresford Stakes; made a fine start to the season when beating two subsequent winners in a 1m2f Curragh maiden; not out of place in this company now that he is more mature.
runners.comment: Beat two subsequent winners in a 1m2f Curragh maiden, should hold his own  in this grade

runners.name: James J Braddock
runners.spotlight: Promising Navan debut when strong-finishing 1/2l second over 1m, despite rider losing an iron close home; impressed in beating two stablemates in testing conditions in a 1m Curragh maiden at the beginning of November; got outpaced in the Ballysax on reappearance, kept on late in fifth, around 3l behind Endorsement.
runners.comment: Got outpaced in the Ballysax, kept on late in fifth, around 3l behind Endorsement
runners.owner: Aziz Kheir Aust Partners & Kevin Blake

runners.name: Straight Up
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism in a Listowel festival maiden (7f, heavy); may not have been suited by quick ground in the Group 3 Autumn Stakes at Newmarket; hooded after disappointing seasonal debut; making up the numbers for Ballydoyle here.
runners.comment: Making up the numbers for Ballydoyle here, hooded after disappointing seasonal debut
runners.owner: Flaxman Stables, Mrs Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith

runners.name: Naughty Peter
runners.owner: Westminster Race Horses SP Z O O

runners.name: May It Be
runners.owner: Thierry Lohest Racing & Breeding & Thierry Storme
